content:
You are a Claude-Mem, a specialized observer tool for creating searchable memory FOR FUTURE SESSIONS.

CRITICAL: Record what was LEARNED/BUILT/FIXED/DEPLOYED/CONFIGURED, not what you (the observer) are doing.

You do not have access to tools. All information you need is provided in <observed_from_primary_session> messages. Create observations from what you observe - no investigation needed.

<observed_from_primary_session>
  <user_request>幫我檢視bridge的程式是否有問題</user_request>
  <requested_at>2026-07-11</requested_at>
</observed_from_primary_session>

Your job is to monitor a different Claude Code session happening RIGHT NOW, with the goal of creating observations and progress summaries as the work is being done LIVE by the user. You are NOT the one doing the work - you are ONLY observing and recording what is being built, fixed, deployed, or configured in the other session.

SPATIAL AWARENESS: Tool executions include the working directory (tool_cwd) to help you understand:
- Which repository/project is being worked on
- Where files are located relative to the project root
- How to match requested paths to actual execution paths

WHAT TO RECORD
--------------
Focus on deliverables and capabilities:
- What the system NOW DOES differently (new capabilities)
- What shipped to users/production (features, fixes, configs, docs)
- Changes in technical domains (auth, data, UI, infra, DevOps, docs)

Use verbs like: implemented, fixed, deployed, configured, migrated, optimized, added, refactored

✅ GOOD EXAMPLES (describes what was built):
- "Authentication now supports OAuth2 with PKCE flow"
- "Deployment pipeline runs canary releases with auto-rollback"
- "Database indexes optimized for common query patterns"

❌ BAD EXAMPLES (describes observation process - DO NOT DO THIS):
- "Analyzed authentication implementation and stored findings"
- "Tracked deployment steps and logged outcomes"
- "Monitored database performance and recorded metrics"

WHEN TO SKIP
------------
Skip routine operations:
- Empty status checks
- Package installations with no errors
- Simple file listings
- Repetitive operations you've already documented
- If file related research comes back as empty or not found
- **No output necessary if skipping.**

OUTPUT FORMAT
-------------
Output observations using this XML structure:

```xml
<observation>
  <type>[ bugfix | feature | refactor | change | discovery | decision ]</type>
  <!--
    **type**: MUST be EXACTLY one of these 6 options (no other values allowed):
      - bugfix: something was broken, now fixed
      - feature: new capability or functionality added
      - refactor: code restructured, behavior unchanged
      - change: generic modification (docs, config, misc)
      - discovery: learning about existing system
      - decision: architectural/design choice with rationale
  -->
  <title>[**title**: Short title capturing the core action or topic]</title>
  <subtitle>[**subtitle**: One sentence explanation (max 24 words)]</subtitle>
  <facts>
    <fact>[Concise, self-contained statement]</fact>
    <fact>[Concise, self-contained statement]</fact>
    <fact>[Concise, self-contained statement]</fact>
  </facts>
  <!--
    **facts**: Concise, self-contained statements
Each fact is ONE piece of information
      No pronouns - each fact must stand alone
      Include specific details: filenames, functions, values

**files**: All files touched (full paths from project root)
  -->
  <narrative>[**narrative**: Full context: What was done, how it works, why it matters]</narrative>
  <concepts>
    <concept>[knowledge-type-category]</concept>
    <concept>[knowledge-type-category]</concept>
  </concepts>
  <!--
    **concepts**: 2-5 knowledge-type categories. MUST use ONLY these exact keywords:
      - how-it-works: understanding mechanisms
      - why-it-exists: purpose or rationale
      - what-changed: modifications made
      - problem-solution: issues and their fixes
      - gotcha: traps or edge cases
      - pattern: reusable approach
      - trade-off: pros/cons of a decision

    IMPORTANT: Do NOT include the observation type (change/discovery/decision) as a concept.
    Types and concepts are separate dimensions.
  -->
  <files_read>
    <file>[path/to/file]</file>
    <file>[path/to/file]</file>
  </files_read>
  <files_modified>
    <file>[path/to/file]</file>
    <file>[path/to/file]</file>
  </files_modified>
</observation>
```


IMPORTANT! DO NOT do any work right now other than generating this OBSERVATIONS from tool use messages - and remember that you are a memory agent designed to summarize a DIFFERENT claude code session, not this one.

Never reference yourself or your own actions. Do not output anything other than the observation content formatted in the XML structure above. All other output is ignored by the system, and the system has been designed to be smart about token usage. Please spend your tokens wisely on useful observations.

Remember that we record these observations as a way of helping us stay on track with our progress, and to help us keep important decisions and changes at the forefront of our minds! :) Thank you so much for your help!

MEMORY PROCESSING START
=======================

Your job is to monitor a different Claude Code session happening RIGHT NOW, with the goal of creating observations and progress summaries as the work is being done LIVE by the user. You are NOT the one doing the work - you are ONLY observing and recording what is being built, fixed, deployed, or configured in the other session.

SPATIAL AWARENESS: Tool executions include the working directory (tool_cwd) to help you understand:
- Which repository/project is being worked on
- Where files are located relative to the project root
- How to match requested paths to actual execution paths

WHAT TO RECORD
--------------
Focus on deliverables and capabilities:
- What the system NOW DOES differently (new capabilities)
- What shipped to users/production (features, fixes, configs, docs)
- Changes in technical domains (auth, data, UI, infra, DevOps, docs)

Use verbs like: implemented, fixed, deployed, configured, migrated, optimized, added, refactored

✅ GOOD EXAMPLES (describes what was built):
- "Authentication now supports OAuth2 with PKCE flow"
- "Deployment pipeline runs canary releases with auto-rollback"
- "Database indexes optimized for common query patterns"

❌ BAD EXAMPLES (describes observation process - DO NOT DO THIS):
- "Analyzed authentication implementation and stored findings"
- "Tracked deployment steps and logged outcomes"
- "Monitored database performance and recorded metrics"

WHEN TO SKIP
------------
Skip routine operations:
- Empty status checks
- Package installations with no errors
- Simple file listings
- Repetitive operations you've already documented
- If file related research comes back as empty or not found
- **No output necessary if skipping.**

OUTPUT FORMAT
-------------
Output observations using this XML structure:

```xml
<observation>
  <type>[ bugfix | feature | refactor | change | discovery | decision ]</type>
  <!--
    **type**: MUST be EXACTLY one of these 6 options (no other values allowed):
      - bugfix: something was broken, now fixed
      - feature: new capability or functionality added
      - refactor: code restructured, behavior unchanged
      - change: generic modification (docs, config, misc)
      - discovery: learning about existing system
      - decision: architectural/design choice with rationale
  -->
  <title>[**title**: Short title capturing the core action or topic]</title>
  <subtitle>[**subtitle**: One sentence explanation (max 24 words)]</subtitle>
  <facts>
    <fact>[Concise, self-contained statement]</fact>
    <fact>[Concise, self-contained statement]</fact>
    <fact>[Concise, self-contained statement]</fact>
  </facts>
  <!--
    **facts**: Concise, self-contained statements
Each fact is ONE piece of information
      No pronouns - each fact must stand alone
      Include specific details: filenames, functions, values

**files**: All files touched (full paths from project root)
  -->
  <narrative>[**narrative**: Full context: What was done, how it works, why it matters]</narrative>
  <concepts>
    <concept>[knowledge-type-category]</concept>
    <concept>[knowledge-type-category]</concept>
  </concepts>
  <!--
    **concepts**: 2-5 knowledge-type categories. MUST use ONLY these exact keywords:
      - how-it-works: understanding mechanisms
      - why-it-exists: purpose or rationale
      - what-changed: modifications made
      - problem-solution: issues and their fixes
      - gotcha: traps or edge cases
      - pattern: reusable approach
      - trade-off: pros/cons of a decision

    IMPORTANT: Do NOT include the observation type (change/discovery/decision) as a concept.
    Types and concepts are separate dimensions.
  -->
  <files_read>
    <file>[path/to/file]</file>
    <file>[path/to/file]</file>
  </files_read>
  <files_modified>
    <file>[path/to/file]</file>
    <file>[path/to/file]</file>

</observation>
```

Never reference yourself or your own actions. Do not output anything other than the observation content formatted in the XML structure above. All other output is ignored by the system, and the system has been designed to be smart about token usage. Please spend your tokens wisely on useful observations.

Remember that we record these observations as a way of helping us stay on track with our progress, and to help us keep important decisions and changes at the forefront of our minds! :) Thank you so much for your help!

=======================

attachment.content:
- deep-research: Deep research harness — fan-out web searches, fetch sources, adversarially verify claims, synthesize a cited report. - When the user wants a deep, multi-source, fact-checked research report on any topic. BEFORE invoking, check if the question is specific enough to research directly — if underspecified (e.g., "what car to buy" without budget/use-case/region), ask 2-3 clarifying questions to narrow scope. Then pass the refined question as args, weaving the answers in.
- dataviz: Use this skill whenever you are about to create ANY chart, graph, plot, dashboard, or data visualization, in ANY output medium — an HTML or React artifact, inline SVG, plotting code in any library (matplotlib, plotly, d3, Recharts, …), an image/PNG you will render and upload, or a chart shared into Slack. Read it BEFORE writing the first line of chart code, choosing chart colors, building a stat tile / meter / KPI row, or laying out a dashboard. Produces visualizations that read as one system — elegant, accessible, consistent in light and dark — using a brand-neutral placeholder palette you swap for your own. Teaches a design-system-agnostic method: a form heuristic, a color formula with a runnable validator, mark specs, and interaction rules. A validated default palette is documented in `references/palette.md` — swap that file's values for your brand's. Triggers on: "chart", "graph", "plot", "data viz", "visualization", "dashboard", "analytics", "visualize data", "categorical colors", "sequential / diverging palette", "stat tile", "sparkline", "heatmap", "legend", "axis", "tooltip", "chart colors", "color by series".
- update-config: Use this skill to configure the Claude Code harness via settings.json. Automated behaviors ("from now on when X", "each time X", "whenever X", "before/after X") require hooks configured in settings.json - the harness executes these, not Claude, so memory/preferences cannot fulfill them. Also use for: permissions ("allow X", "add permission", "move permission to"), env vars ("set X=Y"), hook troubleshooting, or any changes to settings.json/settings.local.json files. Examples: "allow npm commands", "add bq permission to global settings", "move permission to user settings", "set DEBUG=true", "when claude stops show X". For simple settings like theme/model, suggest the /config command.
- keybindings-help: Use when the user wants to customize keyboard shortcuts, rebind keys, add chord bindings, or modify ~/.claude/keybindings.json. Examples: "rebind ctrl+s", "add a chord shortcut", "change the submit key", "customize keybindings".
- verify: Verify that a code change actually does what it's supposed to by exercising it end-to-end and observing behavior — drive the affected flow, not just tests or typecheck. Run before committing nontrivial changes; bootstraps this repo's project verify skill if none exists yet. Don't invoke it on a diff that only touches tests, docs, or other code with no runtime surface to drive (a change to product source always has one) — there's nothing to observe.
- code-review: Review the current diff for correctness bugs and reuse/simplification/efficiency cleanups at the given effort level (low/medium: fewer, high-confidence findings; high→max: broader coverage, may include uncertain findings; ultra: deep multi-agent review in the cloud). Pass --comment to post findings as inline PR comments, or --fix to apply the findings to the working tree after the review.
- simplify: Review the changed code for reuse, simplification, efficiency, and altitude cleanups, then apply the fixes. Quality only — it does not hunt for bugs; use /code-review for that.
- fewer-permission-prompts: Scan your transcripts for common read-only Bash and MCP tool calls, then add a prioritized allowlist to project .claude/settings.json to reduce permission prompts.
- loop: Run a prompt or slash command on a recurring interval (e.g. /loop 5m /foo). Omit the interval to let the model self-pace. - When the user wants to set up a recurring task, poll for status, or run something repeatedly on an interval (e.g. "check the deploy every 5 minutes", "keep running /babysit-prs"). Do NOT invoke for one-off tasks.
- schedule: Create, update, list, or run scheduled cloud agents (routines) that execute on a cron schedule. - When the user wants to schedule a recurring cloud agent, set up automated tasks, create a cron job for Claude Code, or manage their scheduled agents/routines. Also use when the user wants a one-time scheduled run ("run this once at 3pm", "remind me to check X tomorrow").
- claude-api: Reference for the Claude API / Anthropic SDK — model ids, pricing, params, streaming, tool use, MCP, agents, caching, token counting, model migration.
TRIGGER — read BEFORE opening the target file; don't skip because it "looks like a one-liner" — whenever: the prompt names Claude/Anthropic in any form (Claude, Anthropic, Fable, Opus, Sonnet, Haiku, `anthropic`, `@anthropic-ai`, `claude-*`, `us.anthropic.*`, `[1m]`); the user asks about an LLM (pricing/model choice/limits/caching) — never answer from memory; OR the task is LLM-shaped with provider unstated (agent/MCP/tool-definition/multi-agent/RAG/LLM-judge/computer-use; generate/summarize/extract/classify/rewrite/converse over NL; debugging refusals/cutoffs/streaming/tool-calls/tokens).
SKIP only when another provider is being worked on (overrides all triggers): OpenAI/GPT/Gemini/Llama/Mistral/Cohere/Ollama named in the query; OR `grep -rE 'openai|langchain_openai|google.generativeai|genai|mistralai|cohere|ollama'` over the project hits (run this grep FIRST if no provider named — don't Read the file).
- run: Launch and drive this project's app to see a change working. Use when asked to run, start, or screenshot the app, or to confirm a change works in the real app (not just tests). First looks for a project skill that already covers launching the app; otherwise falls back to built-in patterns per project type (CLI, server, TUI, Electron, browser-driven, library).

- security-review: Complete a security review of the pending changes on the current branch

POSIX fs do not exhibit this. Retry with exponential backoff\\n * up to ~500ms, matching what git / electron-builder / pnpm do on\\n * Windows.\\n *\\n * Non-Windows: single attempt, no retry needed.\\n */\\nasync function renameWithWindowsRetry(\\n  tmpPath: string,\\n  targetPath: string,\\n): Promise<void> {\\n  if (process.platform !== \\\"win32\\\") {\\n    await rename(tmpPath, targetPath);\\n    return;\\n  }\\n  const delays = [0, 10, 25, 50, 100, 150, 200]; // sum ≈ 535ms\\n  let lastErr: unknown;\\n  for (const d of delays) {\\n    if (d > 0) await new Promise((r) => setTimeout(r, d));\\n    try {\\n      await rename(tmpPath, targetPath);\\n      return;\\n    } catch (err: any) {\\n      if (err?.code !== \\\"EPERM\\\" && err?.code !== \\\"EBUSY\\\") throw err;\\n      lastErr = err;\\n    }\\n  }\\n  throw lastErr;\\n}\\n\\n/**\\n * Shared atomic JSON helper for bridge state files (.usage.json,\\n * goal-state/*.json, and any future small per-state JSON).\\n *\\n * Design goals, aligned with how schedule-store.ts already behaves:\\n *\\n *   1. Corruption tolerance: ENOENT and malformed-JSON cases return a\\n *      caller-supplied default instead of throwing. Bridge stays up.\\n *   2. Concurrent-write safety: writes to the same path are serialized\\n *      via a per-path `writePending` promise chain so overlapping\\n *      write() calls never interleave bytes on disk.\\n *   3. Crash-safe atomicity: writes go to \\\"<path>.tmp-<pid>-<ts>\\\" and\\n *      are then `rename()`'d into place. On NTFS/ext4/APFS same-volume\\n *      rename is atomic — readers see either the old content or the new,\\n *      never a half-written file.\\n *\\n * Not a goal: cross-process locking. The bridge runs as a single\\n * process; the serialization here handles in-process races. If two\\n * bridges pointed at the same SKILL_DIR run simultaneously, last\\n * writer wins — we do not protect against that.\\n */\\n\\n/** In-process serialization map: filePath → pending write chain. */\\nconst writePendingByPath = new Map<string, Promise<void>>();\\n\\n/**\\n * Read a JSON file. ENOENT and parse errors return `fallback()` without\\n * throwing — the caller decides what \\\"empty\\\" means (empty object,\\n * default schema, etc.).\\n *\\n * Malformed-JSON case is logged (console.error) so silent data loss is\\n * visible in the bridge log, but the call still succeeds.\\n */\\nexport async function readJsonOrDefault<T>(\\n  filePath: string,\\n  fallback: () => T,\\n): Promise<T> {\\n  let raw: string;\\n  try {\\n    raw = await readFile(filePath, \\\"utf8\\\");\\n  } catch (err: any) {\\n    if (err?.code === \\\"ENOENT\\\") return fallback();\\n    throw err;\\n  }\\n  if (raw.trim() === \\\"\\\") return fallback();\\n  try {\\n    return JSON.parse(raw) as T;\\n  } catch (err) {\\n    console.error(\\n      `[atomicJson] ${filePath} is not valid JSON; returning fallback. ` +\\n        `Error: ${(err as Error).message}`,\\n    );\\n    return fallback();\\n  }\\n}\\n\\n/**\\n * Atomically write `data` as pretty-printed JSON to `filePath`.\\n *\\n * Steps:\\n *   1. Ensure parent dir exists (mkdir recursive).\\n *   2. Write to \\\"<filePath>.tmp-<pid>-<timestamp>\\\".\\n *   3. rename() the tmp file over the target. Same-volume rename is\\n *      atomic on NTFS / POSIX fs.\\n *\\n * Concurrent calls for the same `filePath` are serialized via an\\n * in-process promise chain so tmp files don't collide and we never\\n * observe a torn write.\\n *\\n * Errors during step 2/3 are propagated; the caller decides whether to\\n * retry or surface to the user. On step-3 failure we best-effort\\n * unlink() the orphan tmp file so /tmp doesn't fill up over time.\\n */\\nexport async function atomicWriteJson(\\n  filePath: string,\\n  data: unknown,\\n): Promise<void> {\\n  const absPath = path.resolve(filePath);\\n  const prev = writePendingByPath.get(absPath) ?? Promise.resolve();\\n  const next = prev.then(async () => {\\n    await mkdir(path.dirname(absPath), { recursive: true });\\n    const tmpPath = `${absPath}.tmp-${process.pid}-${Date.now()}`;\\n    const body = JSON.stringify(data, null, 2) + \\\"\\\\n\\\";\\n    try {\\n      await writeFile(tmpPath, body, \\\"utf8\\\");\\n      await renameWithWindowsRetry(tmpPath, absPath);\\n    } catch (err) {\\n      // Best-effort cleanup; ignore failures since tmpPath may not exist.\\n      await unlink(tmpPath).catch(() => {});\\n      throw err;\\n    }\\n  });\\n  // Attach a catch so an earlier failure doesn't poison subsequent writes:\\n  // each caller awaits its own `next`, but the chain's `prev` for future\\n  // writers should resolve either way.\\n  writePendingByPath.set(\\n    absPath,\\n    next.catch((err) => {\\n      console.error(`[atomicJson] atomicWriteJson(${absPath}) failed:`, err);\\n    }),\\n  );\\n  await next;\\n}\\n\\n/**\\n * Small convenience for code that reads, mutates, and writes back under\\n * the same serialization guarantee. The `update()` callback runs inside\\n * the write chain, so concurrent update() calls on the same path are\\n * serialized read-modify-write — no lost-update race.\\n *\\n * `initial` is used when the file does not exist or is malformed.\\n */\\nexport async function updateJson<T>(\\n  filePath: string,\\n  initial: () => T,\\n  update: (current: T) => T | Promise<T>,\\n): Promise<T> {\\n  const absPath = path.resolve(filePath);\\n  const prev = writePendingByPath.get(absPath) ?? Promise.resolve();\\n  let result: T;\\n  const next = prev.then(async () => {\\n    const current = await readJsonOrDefault<T>(absPath, initial);\\n    const updated = await update(current);\\n    result = updated;\\n    await mkdir(path.dirname(absPath), { recursive: true });\\n    const tmpPath = `${absPath}.tmp-${process.pid}-${Date.now()}`;\\n    const body = JSON.stringify(updated, null, 2) + \\\"\\\\n\\\";\\n    try {\\n      await writeFile(tmpPath, body, \\\"utf8\\\");\\n      await renameWithWindowsRetry(tmpPath, absPath);\\n    } catch (err) {\\n      await unlink(tmpPath).catch(() => {});\\n      throw err;\\n    }\\n  });\\n  writePendingByPath.set(\\n    absPath,\\n    next.catch((err) => {\\n      console.error(`[atomicJson] updateJson(${absPath}) failed:`, err);\\n    }),\\n  );\\n  await next;\\n  // Non-null assertion is safe: if `next` resolved, `result` was assigned.\\n  return result!;\\n}\\n\",\"numLines\":182,\"startLine\":1,\"totalLines\":182}}"</outcome>

  <outcome>"{\"type\":\"text\",\"file\":{\"filePath\":\"G:\\\\AI\\\\telegram-kiro-bridge-main\\\\src\\\\atomicJson.ts\",\"content\":\"import { mkdir, readFile, rename, unlink, writeFile } from \\\"node:fs/promises\\\";\\nimport path from \\\"node:path\\\";\\n\\n/**\\n * Windows-friendly rename: on NTFS, `rename()` can transiently fail with\\n * EPERM/EBUSY when the target file is briefly held open by another\\n * reader (antivirus, indexer, or even our own just-closed readFile on a\\n * slow GC). POSIX fs do not exhibit this. Retry with exponential backoff\\n * up to ~500ms, matching what git / electron-builder / pnpm do on\\n * Windows.\\n *\\n * Non-Windows: single attempt, no retry needed.\\n */\\nasync function renameWithWindowsRetry(\\n  tmpPath: string,\\n  targetPath: string,\\n): Promise<void> {\\n  if (process.platform !== \\\"win32\\\") {\\n    await rename(tmpPath, targetPath);\\n    return;\\n  }\\n  const delays = [0, 10, 25, 50, 100, 150, 200]; // sum ≈ 535ms\\n  let lastErr: unknown;\\n  for (const d of delays) {\\n    if (d > 0) await new Promise((r) => setTimeout(r, d));\\n    try {\\n      await rename(tmpPath, targetPath);\\n      return;\\n    } catch (err: any) {\\n      if (err?.code !== \\\"EPERM\\\" && err?.code !== \\\"EBUSY\\\") throw err;\\n      lastErr = err;\\n    }\\n  }\\n  throw lastErr;\\n}\\n\\n/**\\n * Shared atomic JSON helper for bridge state files (.usage.json,\\n * goal-state/*.json, and any future small per-state JSON).\\n *\\n * Design goals, aligned with how schedule-store.ts already behaves:\\n *\\n *   1. Corruption tolerance: ENOENT and malformed-JSON cases return a\\n *      caller-supplied default instead of throwing. Bridge stays up.\\n *   2. Concurrent-write safety: writes to the same path are serialized\\n *      via a per-path `writePending` promise chain so overlapping\\n *      write() calls never interleave bytes on disk.\\n *   3. Crash-safe atomicity: writes go to \\\"<path>.tmp-<pid>-<ts>\\\" and\\n *      are then `rename()`'d into place. On NTFS/ext4/APFS same-volume\\n *      rename is atomic — readers see either the old content or the new,\\n *      never a half-written file.\\n *\\n * Not a goal: cross-process locking. The bridge runs as a single\\n * process; the serialization here handles in-process races. If two\\n * bridges pointed at the same SKILL_DIR run simultaneously, last\\n * writer wins — we do not protect against that.\\n */\\n\\n/** In-process serialization map: filePath → pending write chain. */\\nconst writePendingByPath = new Map<string, Promise<void>>();\\n\\n/**\\n * Read a JSON file. ENOENT and parse errors return `fallback()` without\\n * throwing — the caller decides what \\\"empty\\\" means (empty object,\\n * default schema, etc.).\\n *\\n * Malformed-JSON case is logged (console.error) so silent data loss is\\n * visible in the bridge log, but the call still succeeds.\\n */\\nexport async function readJsonOrDefault<T>(\\n  filePath: string,\\n  fallback: () => T,\\n): Promise<T> {\\n  let raw: string;\\n  try {\\n    raw = await readFile(filePath, \\\"utf8\\\");\\n  } catch (err: any) {\\n    if (err?.code === \\\"ENOENT\\\") return fallback();\\n    throw err;\\n  }\\n  if (raw.trim() === \\\"\\\") return fallback();\\n  try {\\n    return JSON.parse(raw) as T;\\n  } catch (err) {\\n    console.error(\\n      `[atomicJson] ${filePath} is not valid JSON; returning fallback. ` +\\n        `Error: ${(err as Error).message}`,\\n    );\\n    return fallback();\\n  }\\n}\\n\\n/**\\n * Atomically write `data` as pretty-printed JSON to `filePath`.\\n *\\n * Steps:\\n *   1. Ensure parent dir exists (mkdir recursive).\\n *   2. Write to \\\"<filePath>.tmp-<pid>-<timestamp>\\\".\\n *   3. rename() the tmp file over the target. Same-volume rename is\\n *      atomic on NTFS / POSIX fs.\\n *\\n * Concurrent calls for the same `filePath` are serialized via an\\n * in-process promise chain so tmp files don't collide and we never\\n * observe a torn write.\\n *\\n * Errors during step 2/3 are propagated; the caller decides whether to\\n * retry or surface to the user. On step-3 failure we best-effort\\n * unlink() the orphan tmp file so /tmp doesn't fill up over time.\\n */\\nexport async function atomicWriteJson(\\n  filePath: string,\\n  data: unknown,\\n): Promise<void> {\\n  const absPath = path.resolve(filePath);\\n  const prev = writePendingByPath.get(absPath) ?? Promise.resolve();\\n  const next = prev.then(async () => {\\n    await mkdir(path.dirname(absPath), { recursive: true });\\n    const tmpPath = `${absPath}.tmp-${process.pid}-${Date.now()}`;\\n    const body = JSON.stringify(data, null, 2) + \\\"\\\\n\\\";\\n    try {\\n      await writeFile(tmpPath, body, \\\"utf8\\\");\\n      await renameWithWindowsRetry(tmpPath, absPath);\\n    } catch (err) {\\n      // Best-effort cleanup; ignore failures since tmpPath may not exist.\\n      await unlink(tmpPath).catch(() => {});\\n      throw err;\\n    }\\n  });\\n  // Attach a catch so an earlier failure doesn't poison subsequent writes:\\n  // each caller awaits its own `next`, but the chain's `prev` for future\\n  // writers should resolve either way.\\n  writePendingByPath.set(\\n    absPath,\\n    next.catch((err) => {\\n      console.error(`[atomicJson] atomicWriteJson(${absPath}) failed:`, err);\\n    }),\\n  );\\n  await next;\\n}\\n\\n/**\\n * Small convenience for code that reads, mutates, and writes back under\\n * the same serialization guarantee. The `update()` callback runs inside\\n * the write chain, so concurrent update() calls on the same path are\\n * serialized read-modify-write — no lost-update race.\\n *\\n * `initial` is used when the file does not exist or is malformed.\\n */\\nexport async function updateJson<T>(\\n  filePath: string,\\n  initial: () => T,\\n  update: (current: T) => T | Promise<T>,\\n): Promise<T> {\\n  const absPath = path.resolve(filePath);\\n  const prev = writePendingByPath.get(absPath) ?? Promise.resolve();\\n  let result: T;\\n  const next = prev.then(async () => {\\n    const current = await readJsonOrDefault<T>(absPath, initial);\\n    const updated = await update(current);\\n    result = updated;\\n    await mkdir(path.dirname(absPath), { recursive: true });\\n    const tmpPath = `${absPath}.tmp-${process.pid}-${Date.now()}`;\\n    const body = JSON.stringify(updated, null, 2) + \\\"\\\\n\\\";\\n    try {\\n      await writeFile(tmpPath, body, \\\"utf8\\\");\\n      await renameWithWindowsRetry(tmpPath, absPath);\\n    } catch (err) {\\n      await unlink(tmpPath).catch(() => {});\\n      throw err;\\n    }\\n  });\\n  writePendingByPath.set(\\n    absPath,\\n    next.catch((err) => {\\n      console.error(`[atomicJson] updateJson(${absPath}) failed:`, err);\\n    }),\\n  );\\n  await next;\\n  // Non-null assertion is safe: if `next` resolved, `result` was assigned.\\n  return result!;\\n}\\n\",\"numLines\":182,\"startLine\":1,\"totalLines\":182}}"</outcome>

AskOption[] = [];\\n  const keys = new Set<string>();\\n  let recommendedCount = 0;\\n  for (const raw of parts.slice(1)) {\\n    if (!raw) continue;\\n    let spec = raw;\\n    let recommended = false;\\n    if (spec.startsWith(\\\"*\\\")) {\\n      recommended = true;\\n      spec = spec.slice(1);\\n    }\\n    const eq = spec.indexOf(\\\"=\\\");\\n    if (eq <= 0) return reject(`option ASK_LABEL_SOFT && label.length <= ASK_LABEL_MAX) {\\n      // Soft-cap warning only; see ASK_LABEL_SOFT definition above for rationale.\\n      console.warn(\\n        `[ASK] label soft cap exceeded (got ${label.length}, soft ${ASK_LABEL_SOFT}): key=\\\"${key}\\\" label=\\\"${label}\\\"`,\\n      );\\n    }\\n    if (label.length > ASK_LABEL_MAX) {\\n      return reject(\\n        `key \\\"${key}\\\" label too long (got ${label.length}, max ${ASK_LABEL_MAX}): \\\"${label}\\\"`,\\n      );\\n    }\\n    if (keys.has(key)) return reject(`duplicate key \\\"${key}\\\"`);\\n    keys.add(key);\\n    if (recommended && recommendedCount > 0) recommended = false;\\n    if (recommended) recommendedCount++;\\n    options.push({ key, label, recommended });\\n  }\\n  if (options.length < 1) return reject(\\\"no valid options\\\");\\n  if (options.length > 8) return reject(`too many options (got ${options.length}, max 8)`);\\n  return { questionId, options };\\n}\\n\\n// ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────\\n// Balanced scanner for tokens with free-form reason bodies (GOAL_DONE /\\n// SKILL_USED / RESTART). At each `<<PREFIX:` encountered (first\\n *  match against any registered prefix wins — they don't overlap because\\n *  all prefixes are `<<UPPERCASE:`), we find the matching top-level\\n *  `>>` and hand the inner substring to that prefix's handler.\\n *\\n *  This outer-first strategy is what prevents a nested `<<GOAL_DONE:>>`\\n *  inside a `<<RESTART:...>>` reason from being extracted as a real\\n *  GOAL_DONE: the scanner sees the `<<RESTART:` opener first, walks to\\n *  its balanced closer (tracking depth so inner `<<>>` pairs don't\\n *  prematurely close), and consumes the whole span atomically.\\n *\\n *  Handler semantics (same as `replaceBalancedTokens`):\\n *    • returns string → replaces the full `<<PREFIX:...>>` span\\n *    • returns null  → token left verbatim, scanner continues past it\\n *\\n *  Newlines abort a candidate span (tokens are single-line per spec);\\n *  the scanner falls past the opener and keeps searching.\\n */\\nfunction runBalancedPhase(\\n  text: string,\\n  handlers: Record<string, (inner: string) => string | null>,\\n): string {\\n  const prefixes = Object.keys(handlers);\\n  let out = \\\"\\\";\\n  let cursor = 0;\\n  while (cursor < text.length) {\\n    // Find the earliest occurrence of ANY registered prefix at or after cursor.\\n    let bestStart = -1;\\n    let bestPrefix = \\\"\\\";\\n    for (const p of prefixes) {\\n      const idx = text.indexOf(p, cursor);\\n      if (idx >= 0 && (bestStart < 0 || idx < bestStart)) {\\n        bestStart = idx;\\n        bestPrefix = p;\\n      }\\n    }\\n    if (bestStart < 0) {\\n      out += text.slice(cursor);\\n      break;\\n    }\\n    // Walk for balanced closer starting after the opener.\\n    const innerStart = bestStart + bestPrefix.length;\\n    let depth = 1;\\n    let i = innerStart;\\n    let aborted = false;\\n    let closedAt = -1;\\n    while (i < text.length - 1) {\\n      const c = text.charCodeAt(i);\\n      if (c === 0x0a || c === 0x0d) {\\n        aborted = true;\\n        break;\\n      }\\n      if (c === 0x3c && text.charCodeAt(i + 1) === 0x3c) {\\n        depth++;\\n        i += 2;\\n        continue;\\n      }\\n      if (c === 0x3e && text.charCodeAt(i + 1) === 0x3e) {\\n        depth--;\\n        if (depth === 0) {\\n          closedAt = i;\\n          break;\\n        }\\n        i += 2;\\n        continue;\\n      }\\n      i++;\\n    }\\n    if (aborted || closedAt < 0) {\\n      // No valid balanced span for this opener. Skip past the opener\\n      // itself (not past the whole line) so a later prefix can still\\n      // match after it.\\n      out += text.slice(cursor, bestStart + bestPrefix.length);\\n      cursor = bestStart + bestPrefix.length;\\n      continue;\\n    }\\n    const inner = text.slice(innerStart, closedAt);\\n    const end = closedAt + 2;\\n    const replacement = handlers[bestPrefix](inner);\\n    out += text.slice(cursor, bestStart);\\n    if (replacement === null) {\\n      out += text.slice(bestStart, end);\\n    } else {\\n      out += replacement;\\n    }\\n    cursor = end;\\n  }\\n  return out;\\n}\\n\\n\\n/**\\n * Extract every token class from `rawReply` in one pass. All limits come from\\n * docs/ab-token-protocol.md.\\n *\\n * Dependency injection: SCHEDULE time-parsers are passed via `parsers`\\n * so this module doesn't drag in schedule-store / recurrence graphs.\\n * Pass `null` (or just omit SCHEDULE parsing entirely) if the caller\\n * doesn't care about schedules — SCHEDULE tokens then stay in the text.\\n */\\nexport function transform(\\n  rawReply: string,\\n  parsers: ScheduleParsers | null = null,\\n): TransformedReply {\\n  if (!rawReply) {\\n    return {\\n      text: \\\"\\\",\\n      sendFiles: [],\\n      scheduleTokens: [],\\n      askTokens: [],\\n      skillUsages: [],\\n      goalDone: null,\\n      restartToken: null,\\n      stickerEmotions: [],\\n      skillPropose: null,\\n      wikiQuery: null,\\n      continueToken: false,\\n      specialistProxy: null,\\n      moaPlan: null,\\n    };\\n  }\\n\\n  const { masked, restore } = maskCodeRegions(rawReply);\\n\\n  // Collect payloads in a mutable object while we do the .replace passes.\\n  const sendFiles: string[] = [];\\n  const scheduleTokens: ScheduleTokenEntry[] = [];\\n  const askTokens: AskEntry[] = [];\\n  const skillUsages: SkillUsageEntry[] = [];\\n  const seenSkillNames = new Set<string>();\\n  const seenAskIds = new Set<string>();\\n  let goalDone: GoalDoneEntry | null = null;\\n  let restartToken: RestartTokenEntry | null = null;\\n  let skillPropose: SkillProposeEntry | null = null;\\n  let wikiQuery: WikiQueryEntry | null = null;\\n  let specialistProxy: SpecialistProxyEntry | null = null;\\n  let moaPlan: MoaPlanEntry | null = null;\\n\\n  // ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────\\n  // Phase 1 — balanced-scanner tokens (free-form reason bodies).\\n  // These run FIRST so that when an agent nests another token inside\\n  // a reason (e.g. `<<GOAL_DONE:... <<RESTART:foo>> ...>>`), the outer\\n  // match consumes the whole span including the nested literal. // only first one\\n      const pipe = inner.indexOf(\\\"|\\\");\\n      if (pipe < 0) return null;\\n      const name = inner.slice(0, pipe).trim();\\n      const reason = inner.slice(pipe + 1).trim();\\n      if (!/^[a-z0-9_-]{1,60}$/.test(name)) return null;\\n      if (!reason || reason.length > 500) return null;\\n      skillPropose = { name, reason };\\n      return \\\"\\\";\\n    },\\n    \\\"<<WIKI_QUERY:\\\": (inner) => {\\n      if (wikiQuery !== null) return null; // only first one\\n      const pipe = inner.indexOf(\\\"|\\\");\\n      if (pipe < 0) return null;\\n      const slug = inner.slice(0, pipe).trim();\\n      const summary = inner.slice(pipe + 1).trim();\\n      if (!/^[a-z0-9_-]{1,80}$/.test(slug)) return null;\\n      if (!summary || summary.length > 200) return null;\\n      wikiQuery = { slug, summary };\\n      return \\\"\\\";\\n    },\\n    \\\"<<SPECIALIST_PROXY:\\\": (inner) => {\\n      if (specialistProxy !== null) return null; // only first one\\n      const pipe = inner.indexOf(\\\"|\\\");\\n      if (pipe < 0) return null;\\n      const name = inner.slice(0, pipe).trim();\\n      const message = inner.slice(pipe + 1).trim();\\n      if (!/^[a-zA-Z0-9_-]{1,60}$/.test(name)) return null;\\n      if (!message) return null;\\n      specialistProxy = { name, message };\\n      return \\\"\\\";\\n    },\\n    \\\"<<MOA_PLAN:\\\": (inner) => {\\n      if (moaPlan !== null) return null; // only first one\\n      const raw = inner.trim();\\n      // Must at least look like a JSON object — anything else stays\\n      // visible so the aggregator sees its own malformed emission.\\n      // Balanced-scanner constraints (single line, no bare `>>` inside)\\n      // are part of the token contract in the plan-mode prompt.\\n      if (!raw.startsWith(\\\"{\\\") || !raw.endsWith(\\\"}\\\")) return null;\\n      moaPlan = { raw };\\n      return \\\"\\\";\\n    },\\n  });\\n\\n  // ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────\\n  // Phase 2 — regex-based tokens (structured bodies: path / time-spec\\n  // / id|k=v). These don't nest other tokens so plain regex is fine.\\n  // ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────\\n\\n  // Pre-fix: repair SEND_FILE paths broken by JSON deserialization.\\n  // When the agent emits backslash Windows paths (F:\\\\AI\\\\...), JSON\\n  // serialization in the CLI turns \\\\r → CR, \\\\n → LF, \\\\t → TAB, etc.\\n  // This makes the regex (which stops at \\\\r\\\\n) unable to capture the\\n  // full path. Fix: within <<SEND_FILE:...>> spans, replace control\\n  // chars back to their backslash-letter originals.\\n  const SEND_FILE_REPAIR_RE = /<<SEND_FILE:\\\\s*([\\\\s\\\\S]+?)\\\\s*>>/g;\\n  stripped = stripped.replace(SEND_FILE_REPAIR_RE, (_m, body: string) => {\\n    const repaired = body\\n      .replace(/\\\\r/g, \\\"\\\\\\\\r\\\")\\n      .replace(/\\\\n/g, \\\"\\\\\\\\n\\\")\\n      .replace(/\\\\t/g, \\\"\\\\\\\\t\\\");\\n    return `<<SEND_FILE:${repaired}>>`;\\n  });\\n\\n  // SEND_FILE\\n  stripped = stripped.replace(SEND_FILE_RE, (_m, p: string) => {\\n    sendFiles.push(p.trim());\\n    return \\\"\\\";\\n  });\\n\\n  // STICKER\\n  const stickerEmotions: string[] = [];\\n  stripped = stripped.replace(STICKER_RE, (_m, emotion: string) => {\\n    const key = emotion.trim().toLowerCase();\\n    if (STICKER_MAP[key] && !stickerEmotions.includes(key)) {\\n      stickerEmotions.push(key);\\n    }\\n    return \\\"\\\";\\n  });\\n\\n  // SCHEDULE (only if parsers provided)\\n  if (parsers) {\\n    const now = parsers.now ? parsers.now() : Date.now();\\n    stripped = stripped.replace(SCHEDULE_RE, (match, inner: string) => {\\n      if (scheduleTokens.length >= SCHEDULE_MAX_PER_MSG) return match;\\n      const pipe = inner.indexOf(\\\"|\\\");\\n      if (pipe <= 0) return match;\\n      const timeRaw = inner.slice(0, pipe).trim();\\n      let prompt = inner.slice(pipe + 1).trim();\\n      if (!prompt) return match;\\n      if (prompt.length > SCHEDULE_PROMPT_MAX) {\\n        prompt = prompt.slice(0, SCHEDULE_PROMPT_MAX);\\n      }\\n      const recurrence = parsers.parseRecurrence(timeRaw);\\n      if (recurrence) {\\n        const firstRunAt = parsers.computeNextRunAt(recurrence, now - 1);\\n        if (firstRunAt === null) return match;\\n        scheduleTokens.push({\\n          runAt: firstRunAt,\\n          prompt,\\n          whenLabel: timeRaw,\\n          recurrence,\\n        });\\n        return \\\"\\\";\\n      }\\n      let runAt: number | null = null;\\n      const dur = parsers.parseDuration(timeRaw);\\n      if (dur !== null) {\\n        runAt = now + dur;\\n      } else {\\n        const dt = parsers.parseAbsoluteTime(timeRaw);\\n        if (dt && dt.getTime() > now) runAt = dt.getTime();\\n      }\\n      if (runAt === null) return match;\\n      scheduleTokens.push({ runAt, prompt, whenLabel: timeRaw });\\n      return \\\"\\\";\\n    });\\n  }\\n\\n  // ASK\\n  stripped = stripped.replace(ASK_RE, (match, inner: string) => {\\n    const entry = parseAskInner(inner);\\n    if (!entry) return match;\\n    if (seenAskIds.has(entry.questionId)) {\\n      const idx = askTokens.findIndex((a) => a.questionId === entry.questionId);\\n      if (idx >= 0) askTokens.splice(idx, 1);\\n    }\\n    seenAskIds.add(entry.questionId);\\n    askTokens.push(entry);\\n    return \\\"\\\";\\n  });\\n\\n  // Bare RESTART. Keep this after the balanced phase so `<<RESTART:...>>`\\n  // retains nested-token handling, while the short host-side form also works.\\n  stripped = stripped.replace(RESTART_BARE_RE, (match) => {\\n    if (restartToken !== null) return match;\\n    restartToken = { reason: RESTART_BARE_REASON };\\n    return \\\"\\\";\\n  });\\n\\n  // CONTINUE — bare token, no payload. Agent signals \\\"I have more work\\n  // to do, please give me another turn\\\" without requiring an active /goal.\\n  let continueToken = false;\\n  stripped = stripped.replace(CONTINUE_RE, () => {\\n    continueToken = true;\\n    return \\\"\\\";\\n  });\\n\\n  // Hide trailing unterminated token opener (see helper docs below).\\n  // 2026-05-02: originally this ran only in renderReply() for streaming\\n  // frames, under the assumption final render always receives a fully-\\n  // closed token set. Live testing showed that's wrong — when the agent\\n  // ends its stream mid-token (e.g. abrupt end-of-turn right after\\n  // `<<GOAL_DONE:reason`), the final render's ab7.text still carries\\n  // the raw opener and the user sees `<<GOAL_DONE:...` glued onto the\\n  // message tail. Running hide on transform()'s cleaned output makes\\n  // both paths (streaming + final) share the same UX guarantee.\\n  const hidden = hideTrailingUnterminatedToken(\\n    restore(stripped).replace(/\\\\n{3,}/g, \\\"\\\\n\\\\n\\\").trim(),\\n  );\\n\\n  return {\\n    text: hidden,\\n    sendFiles,\\n    scheduleTokens,\\n    askTokens,\\n    skillUsages,\\n    goalDone,\\n    restartToken,\\n    stickerEmotions,\\n    skillPropose,\\n    wikiQuery,\\n    continueToken,\\n    specialistProxy,\\n    moaPlan,\\n  };\\n}\\n\\n// ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────\\n// Streaming UX helper: hide a trailing unterminated token opener.\\n// ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────\\n// When the agent is mid-write on a token like `<<GOAL_DONE:reason still\\n// being typed…`, transform() can't match the token (no closing `>>`)\\n// and leaves the opener visible in the output text. <summary>
  <request>Code review of telegram-kiro-bridge program for potential issues</request>
  <investigated>Examined codebase structure (78 TypeScript files, ~26k lines total). Surveyed process spawning patterns in acpClient.ts, backup.ts, local-llm.ts, pet-server.ts. Reviewed HTTP server setup in status-server.ts. Analyzed timer usage across 50+ locations. Read atomic file writing logic in atomicJson.ts, core prompt execution in run-prompt.ts (1,739 lines), and timeout handling in moa.ts and specialist.ts.</investigated>
  <learned>Bridge architecture integrates Telegram bot with Claude/ACP agents supporting specialist routing, relay delegation, multi-agent orchestration (MoA), goal tracking, and memory management. acpClient.ts uses shell-aware spawning with Windows quote escaping. status-server.ts binds localhost-only by default for security. atomicJson.ts implements Windows EPERM/EBUSY retry logic. Timeout management uses Promise.race patterns with setTimeout across specialist dispatch and MoA blind review flows.</learned>
  <completed>Initial code survey completed: identified key architectural components (ACP client spawning, status HTTP server, atomic JSON persistence, specialist timeout orchestration). Mapped timer usage patterns and process spawning surface area for deeper security/resource leak analysis.</completed>
  <next_steps>Running multi-agent `/dev-review` workflow with correctness/security/resource-leak perspectives and adversarial verification. Will synthesize findings from parallel review agents covering timeout races, process lifecycle management, file operation atomicity, and server binding security.</next_steps>
  <notes>Codebase shows sophisticated error handling (Windows-specific retry logic, shell command quoting, localhost-only binding). Large run-prompt.ts file (1,739 lines) handles complex orchestration with relay tokens, specialist proxy, draft streaming, and goal continuation - prime candidate for deep review. Multiple timeout patterns using Promise.race suggest careful timeout discipline but need verification for edge cases.</notes>
</summary>

  <outcome>"{\"type\":\"text\",\"file\":{\"filePath\":\"G:\\\\AI\\\\telegram-kiro-bridge-main\\\\src\\\\observerTransformer.ts\",\"content\":\"// Unified bridge-token extraction. Replaces the three separate\\n// extract*Tokens functions that index.ts defined inline. Covers:\\n//\\n//   <<SEND_FILE:abs-path>>      — existing\\n//   <<SCHEDULE:time|prompt>>    — existing\\n//   <<ASK:question_id|...>>     — existing\\n//   <<SKILL_USED:name|reason>>  — new (AB-1)\\n//   <<GOAL_DONE:reason>>        — new (AB-1)\\n//\\n// Token syntax, limits, and semantics are defined by\\n// `docs/ab-token-protocol.md`. This module is the one extraction path —\\n// no parallel implementations anywhere.\\n//\\n// Design notes:\\n//\\n//  • Code-region masking is mandatory for every regex. Agents frequently\\n//    paste token examples inside ``` fenced or `inline` code blocks to\\n//    explain the protocol; without masking those examples would be\\n//    treated as live tokens and accidentally trigger real side effects.\\n//    The mask/restore pair lives here and is private — callers don't\\n//    need to know about sentinels.\\n//\\n//  • ASK parsing keeps its full-featured parseAskInner function inline\\n//    here so SKILL_USED's simpler parser can sit next to it. The\\n//    Telegram-UI layer (buildAskKeyboard / askRegistry / replyWithAsk)\\n//    stays in index.ts — that's not transformation, it's rendering.\\n//\\n//  • SCHEDULE's time-parsing dependencies (parseDuration,\\n//    parseAbsoluteTime, parseRecurrence, computeNextRunAt) are injected\\n//    via the `scheduleParsers` parameter of `transform()` so this file\\n//    doesn't pull in the whole schedule-store module graph.\\n\\nimport type { Recurrence } from \\\"./recurrence.js\\\";\\nimport { STICKER_MAP } from \\\"./stickerMap.js\\\";\\n\\n// ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────\\n// Regexes — one per token. Each is anchored with `<<NAME:` prefix and\\n// terminates at `>>`. Payload body rejects `>` and CRLF so a stray `>`\\n// inside the payload terminates cleanly instead of eating downstream\\n// tokens.\\n// ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────\\nexport const SEND_FILE_RE = /<<SEND_FILE:\\\\s*([^>\\\\r\\\\n]+?)\\\\s*>>/g;\\nexport const SCHEDULE_RE = /<<SCHEDULE:\\\\s*([^>\\\\r\\\\n]+?)\\\\s*>>/g;\\nexport const ASK_RE = /<<ASK:\\\\s*([^>\\\\r\\\\n]+?)\\\\s*>>/g;\\n// 2026-05-02: reason bodies additionally reject `<` so an agent quoting\\n// other tokens inside a reason (e.g. <<GOAL_DONE:describes <<RESTART:foo>>>>)\\n// can't collapse the outer match onto the inner `>>`. SEND_FILE / SCHEDULE\\n// / ASK bodies keep `<` allowed because their payloads (paths / time specs\\n// / key=val) don't typically contain it, and banning `<` there would break\\n// legitimate payloads like URLs.\\nexport const SKILL_USED_RE =\\n  /<<SKILL_USED:\\\\s*([a-zA-Z0-9_-]{1,60})\\\\s*\\\\|\\\\s*([^<>|\\\\r\\\\n]{0,500})\\\\s*>>/g;\\nexport const GOAL_DONE_RE = /<<GOAL_DONE:\\\\s*([^<>|\\\\r\\\\n]{1,500}?)\\\\s*>>/g;\\n// RESTART token (C3, 2026-05-02). `<<RESTART:reason>>` or bare\\n// `<<RESTART>>` asks the bridge to exit(1) so the supervisor respawns us.\\n// The actual guardrail (goal active? cooldown? cap?) lives in runPrompt;\\n// this regex only extracts the payload. Reason is 1-500 chars, rejects\\n// `<` / `>` / `|` / CRLF so malformed payloads (including agents quoting\\n// other tokens inside the reason) fall through as visible text instead\\n// of collapsing the match on a nested `>>`.\\nexport const RESTART_RE = /<<RESTART:\\\\s*([^<>|\\\\r\\\\n]{1,500}?)\\\\s*>>/g;\\nexport const RESTART_BARE_RE = /<<RESTART>>/g;\\nexport const RESTART_BARE_REASON = \\\"agent-requested-restart\\\";\\nexport const STICKER_RE = /<<STICKER:\\\\s*([a-z_]{1,20})\\\\s*>>/g;\\nexport const CONTINUE_RE = /<<CONTINUE>>/g;\\n\\n// ASK sub-regexes (kept here because ASK payload parsing lives here too).\\nconst ASK_ID_RE = /^[a-zA-Z0-9_-]{1,32}$/;\\nconst ASK_KEY_RE = /^[a-zA-Z0-9_-]{1,20}$/;\\nconst ASK_LABEL_MAX = 40;\\n// Soft cap: labels over this width are accepted but logged. The skill\\n// (ms-telegram-ask-button-protocol) mandates >20 -> numbered body list\\n// with short labels. We do not reject here because rejecting would\\n// leave the whole token verbatim in chat; a log line gives operators\\n// something to grep when a user reports crowded/unreadable buttons.\\nconst ASK_LABEL_SOFT = 20;\\n\\n// Token-class limits (match docs/ab-token-protocol.md).\\nexport const SCHEDULE_PROMPT_MAX = 1000;\\nexport const SCHEDULE_MAX_PER_MSG = 3;\\nexport const SKILL_USED_MAX_PER_MSG = 5;\\nexport const GOAL_DONE_MAX_PER_MSG = 1;\\n// RESTART: only honour the first <<RESTART:...>> in one reply. Extra\\n// tokens stay in visible text as a signal to the agent (same idea as\\n// GOAL_DONE extra-token handling).\\nexport const RESTART_MAX_PER_MSG = 1;\\n// 2026-05-03: balanced-body reason cap raised from 200 → 500 and\\n// switched from hard-reject to silent truncate. Live-observed agent\\n// failure mode: when a goal takes 10+ turns to complete, the GOAL_DONE\\n// reason summarising \\\"why I think we're done\\\" routinely runs 300-600\\n// chinese chars. The old 200-char hard reject left the token verbatim\\n// in text, goalDone=null, and the bridge kept scheduling continuation\\n// turns forever (\\\"GOAL_DONE failed to fire\\\" bug fact, 2026-05-03).\\n// Truncate keeps the token functional while preserving a sane upper\\n// bound that fits comfortably in the \\\"✓ Goal achieved: <reason>\\\" line\\n// on Telegram. Same cap applied to SKILL_USED and RESTART for policy\\n// consistency.\\nexport const BALANCED_REASON_MAX = 500;\\n\\n// ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────\\n// Payload interfaces\\n// ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────\\nexport interface AskOption {\\n  key: string;\\n  label: string;\\n  recommended: boolean;\\n}\\n\\nexport interface AskEntry {\\n  questionId: string;\\n  options: AskOption[];\\n}\\n\\nexport interface ScheduleTokenEntry {\\n  runAt: number;\\n  prompt: string;\\n  whenLabel: string;\\n  recurrence?: Recurrence;\\n}\\n\\nexport interface SkillUsageEntry {\\n  name: string;\\n  reason: string;\\n}\\n\\nexport interface GoalDoneEntry {\\n  reason: string;\\n}\\n\\n/** Payload of a valid <<RESTART:reason>> token. C3 (2026-05-02).\\n *\\n * `reason` is trimmed but not otherwise validated here — the guardrail\\n * layer (goalStore.markRestart + runPrompt) decides whether an empty\\n * reason is allowed. We just extract; empty reasons still produce an\\n * entry so the runPrompt layer can surface a proper \\\"empty-reason\\\"\\n * warning to the user instead of the token silently staying in text. */\\nexport interface RestartTokenEntry {\\n  reason: string;\\n}\\n\\n/** Payload of a valid <<SKILL_PROPOSE:name|reason>> token. Agent proposes\\n *  writing a skill; bridge shows an ASK button for user confirmation. */\\nexport interface SkillProposeEntry {\\n  name: string;\\n  reason: string;\\n}\\n\\n/** Payload of a valid <<WIKI_QUERY:slug|summary>> token. AskOption[] = [];\\n  const keys = new Set<string>();\\n  let recommendedCount = 0;\\n  for (const raw of parts.slice(1)) {\\n    if (!raw) continue;\\n    let spec = raw;\\n    let recommended = false;\\n    if (spec.startsWith(\\\"*\\\")) {\\n      recommended = true;\\n      spec = spec.slice(1);\\n    }\\n    const eq = spec.indexOf(\\\"=\\\");\\n    if (eq <= 0) return reject(`option ASK_LABEL_SOFT && label.length <= ASK_LABEL_MAX) {\\n      // Soft-cap warning only; see ASK_LABEL_SOFT definition above for rationale.\\n      console.warn(\\n        `[ASK] label soft cap exceeded (got ${label.length}, soft ${ASK_LABEL_SOFT}): key=\\\"${key}\\\" label=\\\"${label}\\\"`,\\n      );\\n    }\\n    if (label.length > ASK_LABEL_MAX) {\\n      return reject(\\n        `key \\\"${key}\\\" label too long (got ${label.length}, max ${ASK_LABEL_MAX}): \\\"${label}\\\"`,\\n      );\\n    }\\n    if (keys.has(key)) return reject(`duplicate key \\\"${key}\\\"`);\\n    keys.add(key);\\n    if (recommended && recommendedCount > 0) recommended = false;\\n    if (recommended) recommendedCount++;\\n    options.push({ key, label, recommended });\\n  }\\n  if (options.length < 1) return reject(\\\"no valid options\\\");\\n  if (options.length > 8) return reject(`too many options (got ${options.length}, max 8)`);\\n  return { questionId, options };\\n}\\n\\n// ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────\\n// Balanced scanner for tokens with free-form reason bodies (GOAL_DONE /\\n// SKILL_USED / RESTART). At each `<<PREFIX:` encountered (first\\n *  match against any registered prefix wins — they don't overlap because\\n *  all prefixes are `<<UPPERCASE:`), we find the matching top-level\\n *  `>>` and hand the inner substring to that prefix's handler.\\n *\\n *  This outer-first strategy is what prevents a nested `<<GOAL_DONE:>>`\\n *  inside a `<<RESTART:...>>` reason from being extracted as a real\\n *  GOAL_DONE: the scanner sees the `<<RESTART:` opener first, walks to\\n *  its balanced closer (tracking depth so inner `<<>>` pairs don't\\n *  prematurely close), and consumes the whole span atomically.\\n *\\n *  Handler semantics (same as `replaceBalancedTokens`):\\n *    • returns string → replaces the full `<<PREFIX:...>>` span\\n *    • returns null  → token left verbatim, scanner continues past it\\n *\\n *  Newlines abort a candidate span (tokens are single-line per spec);\\n *  the scanner falls past the opener and keeps searching.\\n */\\nfunction runBalancedPhase(\\n  text: string,\\n  handlers: Record<string, (inner: string) => string | null>,\\n): string {\\n  const prefixes = Object.keys(handlers);\\n  let out = \\\"\\\";\\n  let cursor = 0;\\n  while (cursor < text.length) {\\n    // Find the earliest occurrence of ANY registered prefix at or after cursor.\\n    let bestStart = -1;\\n    let bestPrefix = \\\"\\\";\\n    for (const p of prefixes) {\\n      const idx = text.indexOf(p, cursor);\\n      if (idx >= 0 && (bestStart < 0 || idx < bestStart)) {\\n        bestStart = idx;\\n        bestPrefix = p;\\n      }\\n    }\\n    if (bestStart < 0) {\\n      out += text.slice(cursor);\\n      break;\\n    }\\n    // Walk for balanced closer starting after the opener.\\n    const innerStart = bestStart + bestPrefix.length;\\n    let depth = 1;\\n    let i = innerStart;\\n    let aborted = false;\\n    let closedAt = -1;\\n    while (i < text.length - 1) {\\n      const c = text.charCodeAt(i);\\n      if (c === 0x0a || c === 0x0d) {\\n        aborted = true;\\n        break;\\n      }\\n      if (c === 0x3c && text.charCodeAt(i + 1) === 0x3c) {\\n        depth++;\\n        i += 2;\\n        continue;\\n      }\\n      if (c === 0x3e && text.charCodeAt(i + 1) === 0x3e) {\\n        depth--;\\n        if (depth === 0) {\\n          closedAt = i;\\n          break;\\n        }\\n        i += 2;\\n        continue;\\n      }\\n      i++;\\n    }\\n    if (aborted || closedAt < 0) {\\n      // No valid balanced span for this opener. Skip past the opener\\n      // itself (not past the whole line) so a later prefix can still\\n      // match after it.\\n      out += text.slice(cursor, bestStart + bestPrefix.length);\\n      cursor = bestStart + bestPrefix.length;\\n      continue;\\n    }\\n    const inner = text.slice(innerStart, closedAt);\\n    const end = closedAt + 2;\\n    const replacement = handlers[bestPrefix](inner);\\n    out += text.slice(cursor, bestStart);\\n    if (replacement === null) {\\n      out += text.slice(bestStart, end);\\n    } else {\\n      out += replacement;\\n    }\\n    cursor = end;\\n  }\\n  return out;\\n}\\n\\n\\n/**\\n * Extract every token class from `rawReply` in one pass. All limits come from\\n * docs/ab-token-protocol.md.\\n *\\n * Dependency injection: SCHEDULE time-parsers are passed via `parsers`\\n * so this module doesn't drag in schedule-store / recurrence graphs.\\n * Pass `null` (or just omit SCHEDULE parsing entirely) if the caller\\n * doesn't care about schedules — SCHEDULE tokens then stay in the text.\\n */\\nexport function transform(\\n  rawReply: string,\\n  parsers: ScheduleParsers | null = null,\\n): TransformedReply {\\n  if (!rawReply) {\\n    return {\\n      text: \\\"\\\",\\n      sendFiles: [],\\n      scheduleTokens: [],\\n      askTokens: [],\\n      skillUsages: [],\\n      goalDone: null,\\n      restartToken: null,\\n      stickerEmotions: [],\\n      skillPropose: null,\\n      wikiQuery: null,\\n      continueToken: false,\\n      specialistProxy: null,\\n      moaPlan: null,\\n    };\\n  }\\n\\n  const { masked, restore } = maskCodeRegions(rawReply);\\n\\n  // Collect payloads in a mutable object while we do the .replace passes.\\n  const sendFiles: string[] = [];\\n  const scheduleTokens: ScheduleTokenEntry[] = [];\\n  const askTokens: AskEntry[] = [];\\n  const skillUsages: SkillUsageEntry[] = [];\\n  const seenSkillNames = new Set<string>();\\n  const seenAskIds = new Set<string>();\\n  let goalDone: GoalDoneEntry | null = null;\\n  let restartToken: RestartTokenEntry | null = null;\\n  let skillPropose: SkillProposeEntry | null = null;\\n  let wikiQuery: WikiQueryEntry | null = null;\\n  let specialistProxy: SpecialistProxyEntry | null = null;\\n  let moaPlan: MoaPlanEntry | null = null;\\n\\n  // ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────\\n  // Phase 1 — balanced-scanner tokens (free-form reason bodies).\\n  // These run FIRST so that when an agent nests another token inside\\n  // a reason (e.g. `<<GOAL_DONE:... <<RESTART:foo>> ...>>`), the outer\\n  // match consumes the whole span including the nested literal. // only first one\\n      const pipe = inner.indexOf(\\\"|\\\");\\n      if (pipe < 0) return null;\\n      const name = inner.slice(0, pipe).trim();\\n      const reason = inner.slice(pipe + 1).trim();\\n      if (!/^[a-z0-9_-]{1,60}$/.test(name)) return null;\\n      if (!reason || reason.length > 500) return null;\\n      skillPropose = { name, reason };\\n      return \\\"\\\";\\n    },\\n    \\\"<<WIKI_QUERY:\\\": (inner) => {\\n      if (wikiQuery !== null) return null; // only first one\\n      const pipe = inner.indexOf(\\\"|\\\");\\n      if (pipe < 0) return null;\\n      const slug = inner.slice(0, pipe).trim();\\n      const summary = inner.slice(pipe + 1).trim();\\n      if (!/^[a-z0-9_-]{1,80}$/.test(slug)) return null;\\n      if (!summary || summary.length > 200) return null;\\n      wikiQuery = { slug, summary };\\n      return \\\"\\\";\\n    },\\n    \\\"<<SPECIALIST_PROXY:\\\": (inner) => {\\n      if (specialistProxy !== null) return null; // only first one\\n      const pipe = inner.indexOf(\\\"|\\\");\\n      if (pipe < 0) return null;\\n      const name = inner.slice(0, pipe).trim();\\n      const message = inner.slice(pipe + 1).trim();\\n      if (!/^[a-zA-Z0-9_-]{1,60}$/.test(name)) return null;\\n      if (!message) return null;\\n      specialistProxy = { name, message };\\n      return \\\"\\\";\\n    },\\n    \\\"<<MOA_PLAN:\\\": (inner) => {\\n      if (moaPlan !== null) return null; // only first one\\n      const raw = inner.trim();\\n      // Must at least look like a JSON object — anything else stays\\n      // visible so the aggregator sees its own malformed emission.\\n      // Balanced-scanner constraints (single line, no bare `>>` inside)\\n      // are part of the token contract in the plan-mode prompt.\\n      if (!raw.startsWith(\\\"{\\\") || !raw.endsWith(\\\"}\\\")) return null;\\n      moaPlan = { raw };\\n      return \\\"\\\";\\n    },\\n  });\\n\\n  // ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────\\n  // Phase 2 — regex-based tokens (structured bodies: path / time-spec\\n  // / id|k=v). These don't nest other tokens so plain regex is fine.\\n  // ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────\\n\\n  // Pre-fix: repair SEND_FILE paths broken by JSON deserialization.\\n  // When the agent emits backslash Windows paths (F:\\\\AI\\\\...), JSON\\n  // serialization in the CLI turns \\\\r → CR, \\\\n → LF, \\\\t → TAB, etc.\\n  // This makes the regex (which stops at \\\\r\\\\n) unable to capture the\\n  // full path. Fix: within <<SEND_FILE:...>> spans, replace control\\n  // chars back to their backslash-letter originals.\\n  const SEND_FILE_REPAIR_RE = /<<SEND_FILE:\\\\s*([\\\\s\\\\S]+?)\\\\s*>>/g;\\n  stripped = stripped.replace(SEND_FILE_REPAIR_RE, (_m, body: string) => {\\n    const repaired = body\\n      .replace(/\\\\r/g, \\\"\\\\\\\\r\\\")\\n      .replace(/\\\\n/g, \\\"\\\\\\\\n\\\")\\n      .replace(/\\\\t/g, \\\"\\\\\\\\t\\\");\\n    return `<<SEND_FILE:${repaired}>>`;\\n  });\\n\\n  // SEND_FILE\\n  stripped = stripped.replace(SEND_FILE_RE, (_m, p: string) => {\\n    sendFiles.push(p.trim());\\n    return \\\"\\\";\\n  });\\n\\n  // STICKER\\n  const stickerEmotions: string[] = [];\\n  stripped = stripped.replace(STICKER_RE, (_m, emotion: string) => {\\n    const key = emotion.trim().toLowerCase();\\n    if (STICKER_MAP[key] && !stickerEmotions.includes(key)) {\\n      stickerEmotions.push(key);\\n    }\\n    return \\\"\\\";\\n  });\\n\\n  // SCHEDULE (only if parsers provided)\\n  if (parsers) {\\n    const now = parsers.now ? parsers.now() : Date.now();\\n    stripped = stripped.replace(SCHEDULE_RE, (match, inner: string) => {\\n      if (scheduleTokens.length >= SCHEDULE_MAX_PER_MSG) return match;\\n      const pipe = inner.indexOf(\\\"|\\\");\\n      if (pipe <= 0) return match;\\n      const timeRaw = inner.slice(0, pipe).trim();\\n      let prompt = inner.slice(pipe + 1).trim();\\n      if (!prompt) return match;\\n      if (prompt.length > SCHEDULE_PROMPT_MAX) {\\n        prompt = prompt.slice(0, SCHEDULE_PROMPT_MAX);\\n      }\\n      const recurrence = parsers.parseRecurrence(timeRaw);\\n      if (recurrence) {\\n        const firstRunAt = parsers.computeNextRunAt(recurrence, now - 1);\\n        if (firstRunAt === null) return match;\\n        scheduleTokens.push({\\n          runAt: firstRunAt,\\n          prompt,\\n          whenLabel: timeRaw,\\n          recurrence,\\n        });\\n        return \\\"\\\";\\n      }\\n      let runAt: number | null = null;\\n      const dur = parsers.parseDuration(timeRaw);\\n      if (dur !== null) {\\n        runAt = now + dur;\\n      } else {\\n        const dt = parsers.parseAbsoluteTime(timeRaw);\\n        if (dt && dt.getTime() > now) runAt = dt.getTime();\\n      }\\n      if (runAt === null) return match;\\n      scheduleTokens.push({ runAt, prompt, whenLabel: timeRaw });\\n      return \\\"\\\";\\n    });\\n  }\\n\\n  // ASK\\n  stripped = stripped.replace(ASK_RE, (match, inner: string) => {\\n    const entry = parseAskInner(inner);\\n    if (!entry) return match;\\n    if (seenAskIds.has(entry.questionId)) {\\n      const idx = askTokens.findIndex((a) => a.questionId === entry.questionId);\\n      if (idx >= 0) askTokens.splice(idx, 1);\\n    }\\n    seenAskIds.add(entry.questionId);\\n    askTokens.push(entry);\\n    return \\\"\\\";\\n  });\\n\\n  // Bare RESTART. Keep this after the balanced phase so `<<RESTART:...>>`\\n  // retains nested-token handling, while the short host-side form also works.\\n  stripped = stripped.replace(RESTART_BARE_RE, (match) => {\\n    if (restartToken !== null) return match;\\n    restartToken = { reason: RESTART_BARE_REASON };\\n    return \\\"\\\";\\n  });\\n\\n  // CONTINUE — bare token, no payload. Agent signals \\\"I have more work\\n  // to do, please give me another turn\\\" without requiring an active /goal.\\n  let continueToken = false;\\n  stripped = stripped.replace(CONTINUE_RE, () => {\\n    continueToken = true;\\n    return \\\"\\\";\\n  });\\n\\n  // Hide trailing unterminated token opener (see helper docs below).\\n  // 2026-05-02: originally this ran only in renderReply() for streaming\\n  // frames, under the assumption final render always receives a fully-\\n  // closed token set. Live testing showed that's wrong — when the agent\\n  // ends its stream mid-token (e.g. abrupt end-of-turn right after\\n  // `<<GOAL_DONE:reason`), the final render's ab7.text still carries\\n  // the raw opener and the user sees `<<GOAL_DONE:...` glued onto the\\n  // message tail. Running hide on transform()'s cleaned output makes\\n  // both paths (streaming + final) share the same UX guarantee.\\n  const hidden = hideTrailingUnterminatedToken(\\n    restore(stripped).replace(/\\\\n{3,}/g, \\\"\\\\n\\\\n\\\").trim(),\\n  );\\n\\n  return {\\n    text: hidden,\\n    sendFiles,\\n    scheduleTokens,\\n    askTokens,\\n    skillUsages,\\n    goalDone,\\n    restartToken,\\n    stickerEmotions,\\n    skillPropose,\\n    wikiQuery,\\n    continueToken,\\n    specialistProxy,\\n    moaPlan,\\n  };\\n}\\n\\n// ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────\\n// Streaming UX helper: hide a trailing unterminated token opener.\\n// ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────\\n// When the agent is mid-write on a token like `<<GOAL_DONE:reason still\\n// being typed…`, transform() can't match the token (no closing `>>`)\\n// and leaves the opener visible in the output text. string) => string } {\\n  const slots: string[] = [];\\n  const masked = text.replace(/```[\\\\s\\\\S]*?```|`[^`\\\\n]*`/g, (m) => {\\n    const idx = slots.length;\\n    slots.push(m);\\n    return };\\n}\\n\\nexport function isRelayDelegatePlaceholderGoal(goal: string): boolean {\\n  const g = goal.trim();\\n  return g === \\\"\\\" || g === \\\"...\\\" || g === \\\"…\\\" || /^<[^>\\\\r\\\\n]*>?$/.test(g);\\n}\\n\\nexport function captureRelayDelegateTokensFromText(buffer: string, existing: string[] = []): string[] {\\n  const next = [...existing];\\n  const { masked, restore } = maskCodeRegionsForRelayCapture(buffer);\\n  let m: RegExpExecArray | null;\\n  RELAY_DELEGATE_RE.lastIndex = 0;\\n  while ((m = RELAY_DELEGATE_RE.exec(masked)) !== null) {\\n    const goal next;\\n}\\n\\n/**\\n * Resume 候選決策：flag on + registry 有記錄 + agent command 一致 +\\n * backend key 一致才回 sessionId。任何不一致都靜默走 fresh（防呆優先）。\\n * 獨立成 export 函式是為了 smoke test 可直測，不需拉起 SessionManager。\\n */\\nexport async function resolveResumeCandidate(\\n  chatId: number,\\n  backend: AcpBackendDef | undefined,\\n  agentCommand: string,\\n): Promise<string | undefined> {\\n  if (rec.backendKey !== (backend?.key ?? \\\"\\\")) return undefined;\\n  return rec.sessionId;\\n}\\n\\nexport interface HistoryEntry {\\n  role: \\\"user\\\" | \\\"assistant\\\";\\n  text: string;\\n  timestamp: number;\\n}\\n\\n/** One tool call's progress within the in-flight turn. */\\nexport interface ToolEvent {\\n  /** ACP toolCallId when present, else a synthetic id for matching updates. */\\n  id: string;\\n  /** Human-readable tool name/title. */\\n  name: string;\\n  status: \\\"running\\\" | \\\"completed\\\" | \\\"failed\\\";\\n  /** Date.now() at tool_call start. */\\n  startedAt: number;\\n  /** Wall-clock ms once completed/failed; undefined while running. */\\n  elapsedMs?: number;\\n}\\n\\nconst TOOL_STATUS_EMOJI = {\\n  running: \\\"⚙️\\\",\\n  completed: \\\"✅\\\",\\n  failed: \\\"❌\\\",\\n} as const;\\n\\n/**\\n * Mark a running tool as completed/failed, stamping elapsed time. Matches by\\n * ACP toolCallId; if the update carries no id (or none matches), falls back to\\n * the most recent still-running entry so the line still transitions.\\n */\\nfunction markToolDone(\\n  session: ChatSession,\\n  updId: string | undefined,\\n  status: \\\"completed\\\" | \\\"failed\\\",\\n): void {\\n  let entry = updId\\n    ? session.toolTimeline.find((t) => t.id === updId && t.status === \\\"running\\\")\\n    : undefined;\\n  if (!entry) {\\n    for (let i = session.toolTimeline.length - 1; i >= 0; i--) {\\n      if (session.toolTimeline[i].status === \\\"running\\\") {\\n        entry = session.toolTimeline[i];\\n        break;\\n      }\\n    }\\n  }\\n  if (entry) {\\n    entry.status = status;\\n    entry.elapsedMs = Date.now() - entry.startedAt;\\n  }\\n}\\n\\n/**\\n * Lines shown as the tool-progress prefix of the streamed reply: one line per\\n * tool (latest 5) as `<emoji> <name> (<secs>s)`, followed by any advisory\\n * lines (reflexion hints) carried in {@link ChatSession.toolLog}.\\n */\\n// Tool 行是進度指示器不是審計記錄：title 是 adapter 原文（可能是整條\\n// Bash 命令或絕對路徑，甚至含換行），render 層單行化 + 截斷；\\n// toolTimeline 保留原始資料不加工。\\nconst TOOL_LINE_NAME_MAX = 64;\\n\\nexport function toolPrefixLines(session: ChatSession): string[] {\\n  const lines = session.toolTimeline.slice(-5).map((t) => {\\n    const secs =\\n      t.status !== \\\"running\\\" && t.elapsedMs !== undefined\\n        ? ` (${(t.elapsedMs / 1000).toFixed(1)}s)`\\n        : \\\"\\\";\\n    let name = t.name.replace(/\\\\s*[\\\\r\\\\n]+\\\\s*/g, \\\" \\\");\\n    if (name.length > TOOL_LINE_NAME_MAX) {\\n      name = name.slice(0, TOOL_LINE_NAME_MAX - 1) + \\\"…\\\";\\n    }\\n    return `${TOOL_STATUS_EMOJI[t.status]} ${name}${secs}`;\\n  });\\n  return [...lines, ...session.toolLog];\\n}\\n\\n/**\\n * Per-Telegram-chat session. Each chat owns its own Kiro ACP subprocess,\\n * so conversations stay isolated.\\n */\\nexport interface ChatSession {\\n  chatId: number;\\n  userId?: number;\\n  client: Provider;\\n  /** Which provider type is active for this session. */\\n  activeProvider: ProviderType;\\n  /**\\n   * Which ACP backend (acp-providers.json key) this session's subprocess was\\n   * spawned from. undefined = the .env agent (no /agent switch yet) or a\\n   * non-ACP provider.\\n   */\\n  acpBackend?: string;\\n  sessionId: string;\\n  lastUsed: number;\\n  /** Accumulated assistant text for the in-flight turn. */\\n  buffer: string;\\n  /** Accumulated thinking text for the in-flight turn (local model only). */\\n  thinkingBuffer?: string;\\n  /**\\n   * Non-tool status lines for the in-flight turn (e.g. reflexion hints).\\n   * Per-tool progress now lives in {@link toolTimeline}; this only carries\\n   * the extra advisory lines that render after the tool list.\\n   */\\n  toolLog: string[];\\n  /**\\n   * Per-tool progress for the in-flight turn. Stashed so that if THIS\\n   * session turns out trivial (< config.archiveMinTurns), archiveOnClose can\\n   * carry the prior, more-substantive archive forward instead of clobbering it\\n   * with a throwaway one. undefined if there was no prior archive.\\n   */\\n  priorArchive?: SessionArchive;\\n}\\n\\nexport class SessionManager {\\n  private sessions = new Map<number, ChatSession>();\\n  private sweeper: NodeJS.Timeout;\\n  /**\\n   * Per-chat ACP backend selection (/agent). Wired in index.ts to the run-prompt flusher so orphan\\n   * delivery reuses the SAME reply transform / render / token path as the\\n   * normal request→response flow. `text` is the raw accumulated buffer.\\n   */\\n  public onOrphanTurn?: (session: ChatSession, text: string) => void | Promise<void>;\\n\\n  constructor() {\\n    this.sweeper = setInterval(() => this.sweepIdle(), 60_000);\\n    this.sweeper.unref?.();\\n    // Fire-and-forget: load persisted /agent choices. If the file doesn't\\n    // exist or is corrupt we just start fresh (same as before).\\n    loadPinnedAgents().then((map) => {\\n      for (const [k, v] of map) {\\n        if (!this.acpBackendByChat.has(k)) this.acpBackendByChat.set(k, v);\\n      }\\n    }).catch(() => {});\\n  }\\n\\n  async get(chatId: number, userId?: number): Promise<ChatSession> {\\n    const existing = this.sessions.get(chatId);\\n    if (existing && !existing.client.isClosed) {\\n      existing.lastUsed = Date.now();\\n      return existing;\\n    }\\n    return this.create(chatId, userId);\\n  }\\n\\n  private async create(chatId: number, userId?: number, providerType?: ProviderType): Promise<ChatSession> {\\n    // Inject TG_ACTIVE_USER_ID so MCP children (mcp-memory) know whose\\n    // facts file to write. MEMORY_DIR is passed through too so the mcp\\n    // server doesn't need to re-read .env.\\n    const env: Record<string, string> = { MEMORY_DIR: config.memoryDir };\\n    if (userId) env.TG_ACTIVE_USER_ID = String(userId);\\n\\n    const activeType = providerType ?? config.defaultProvider;\\n\\n    // Resolve the pinned ACP backend (/agent). Looked up on EVERY create —\\n    // including crash-recreates via get() — so the chosen backend sticks.\\n    let backend: AcpBackendDef | undefined;\\n    if (activeType === \\\"acp\\\") {\\n      const key = this.acpBackendByChat.get(chatId);\\n      if (key) {\\n        backend = loadAcpProviders()[key];\\n        if (!backend) {\\n          console.warn(\\n            `[session ${chatId}] pinned ACP backend \\\"${key}\\\" not in acp-providers.json — falling back to .env agent`,\\n          );\\n          this.acpBackendByChat.delete(chatId);\\n          savePinnedAgents(this.acpBackendByChat).catch(() => {});\\n        }\\n      }\\n    }\\n\\n    const facts = userId ? await loadFacts(userId) : \\\"\\\";\\n\\n    // Try to compress recent facts via local LLM (graceful fallback if unavailable)\\n    let compressed: string | null = null;\\n    if (facts && activeType !== \\\"local\\\") {\\n      const lines = facts.split(/\\\\r?\\\\n/).filter(l => l.trim().length > 0);\\n      const tail = config.memoryPreambleTail;\\n      if (lines.length > tail) {\\n        const { compressFactsForPreamble: compress } = await import(\\\"./local-llm.js\\\");\\n        compressed = await compress(lines.slice(-tail));\\n      }\\n    }\\n    const breakdown = activeType === \\\"local\\\"\\n      ? buildLocalPreamble(facts, userId)\\n      : buildPreambleWithBreakdown(facts, userId, { compressed, agentCommand: backend?.command });\\n\\n    // Inject working-state from previous session if available (one-shot).\\n    const wsPath = path.join(config.memoryDir, `working-state-${chatId}.md`);\\n    let workingStateBlock = \\\"\\\";\\n    try {\\n      const ws = await readFile(wsPath, \\\"utf8\\\");\\n      workingStateBlock =\\n        \\\"\\\\n[Previous session working state — use as context to resume work]\\\\n\\\" +\\n        ws +\\n        \\\"\\\\nResume from `next_action` above. Do not ask what was done last time.\\\\n\\\" +\\n        \\\"[End working state]\\\\n\\\\n\\\";\\n      await unlink(wsPath);\\n      console.log(`[session] injected working-state for chat ${chatId}`);\\n    } catch { /* file doesn't exist — normal */ }\\n\\n    // Inject structured session archive restoration (complements working-state\\n    // with turn-level context, goal state, and session stats).\\n    let archiveBlock = \\\"\\\";\\n    let priorArchive: SessionArchive | undefined;\\n    try {\\n      const { loadArchive, buildRestorationBlock, deleteArchive, isArchiveStale } = await import(\\\"./session-archive.js\\\");\\n      const archive = await loadArchive(chatId);\\n      if (archive) {\\n        if (isArchiveStale(archive, config.archiveMaxAgeHours, Date.now())) {\\n          const ageH = Math.round((Date.now() - new Date(archive.archivedAt).getTime()) / 3600_000);\\n          // Too old to be a useful handoff. await deleteArchive(chatId);\\n          console.log(`[session] discarded stale archive for chat ${chatId} (age ${ageH}h > ${config.archiveMaxAgeHours}h)`);\\n        } else {\\n          archiveBlock = \\\"\\\\n\\\" + buildRestorationBlock(archive) + \\\"\\\\n\\\\n\\\";\\n          await deleteArchive(chatId);\\n          priorArchive = archive; // stash for trivial-session carry-forward on close\\n          console.log(`[session] injected session archive for chat ${chatId} (${archive.stats.turnCount} turns, closed=${archive.closeReason})`);\\n        }\\n      }\\n    } catch (err) {\\n      console.error(`[session] loadArchive failed for chat ${chatId}:`, (err as Error).message);\\n    }\\n\\n    // Inject pending dream suggestions (High Priority from last /dream run).\\n    // Read-only：STATE.md 由 dream 每晚重寫，這裡不刪。48h 新鮮度閘門防止\\n    // 注入陳年建議。Frozen-snapshot 合規：只在 create() 組一次，隨 preamble 凍結。\\n    let dreamStateBlock = \\\"\\\";\\n    try {\\n      const { hp.lastRun.getTime() < 48 * 3600_000;\\n      if (hp && fresh && hp.items.length > 0) {\\n        dreamStateBlock =\\n          \\\"\\\\n[Pending dream suggestions — 上次 /dream 維運的 High Priority 待辦]\\\\n\\\" +\\n          hp.items.map((it) => `- ${it}`).join(\\\"\\\\n\\\") +\\n          \\\"\\\\n當使用者說「處理建議」「dream 建議」或類似語句時，即指上述項目。\\\\n\\\" +\\n          `完整三層清單（含 Watch List）在 ${loopStatePath()}。處理完某項後，更新該檔移除或標記已處理。\\\\n` +\\n          \\\"[End pending dream suggestions]\\\\n\\\\n\\\";\\n        console.log(`[session] injected ${hp.items.length} pending dream suggestion(s) for chat ${chatId}`);\\n      }\\n    } catch { /* fail-open：STATE.md 缺失或格式不符不影響 session 建立 */ }\\n\\n    const relayTaskBlock = await formatActiveRelayTasksForPreamble(chatId);\\n    const preambleText = breakdown.text + workingStateBlock + archiveBlock + dreamStateBlock + relayTaskBlock;\\n    const agentCommand = backend?.command ?? config.agent.command;\\n    const resumeSessionId = activeType === \\\"acp\\\"\\n      ? await resolveResumeCandidate(chatId, backend, agentCommand)\\n      : undefined;\\n    const provider = this.createProvider(activeType, env, preambleText, backend,   charsOut: 0,\\n      memoryPreamble: preambleText,\\n      preambleBreakdown: {\\n        total: preambleText.length,\\n        toolNotes: breakdown.toolNotes,\\n        envBlock:        if (this.onUnexpectedExit) {\\n          this.onUnexpectedExit(session, code).catch((e) =>\\n            console.error(`[session ${chatId}] onUnexpectedExit failed:`, e),\\n          );\\n session.resumed = true;\\n      console.log(`[session ${chatId}] resumed ACP session ${session.sessionId}`);\\n    }\\n    // Registry 生命週期：flag on 時記錄本 session（bridge 整個 crash 也能恢復）\\n    if session;\\n  }\\n\\n  /** Factory: create the right Provider based on type. */\\n  private createProvider(type: ProviderType, env: Record<string, string>, systemPrompt?: string, backend?:
