[\\\"memory\\\", \\\"notebooklm\\\", \\\"google\\\"] : undefined,\\n    });\\n  }\\n\\n  /**\\n   * Switch the active provider for a chat. Closes the old session and\\n   * creates a new one with the specified provider type.\\n   * Returns the new session.\\n   */\\n  async switchProvider(chatId: number, type: ProviderType, userId?: number): Promise<ChatSession> {\\n    const existing = this.sessions.get(chatId);\\n    if (existing) {\\n      this.sessions.delete(chatId);\\n      await existing.client.close().catch(() => {});\\n    }\\n    return this.create(chatId, userId, type);\\n  }\\n\\n  /**\\n   * Switch the ACP backend for a chat (/agent) with conversation handoff.\\n   * Reuses the full drop() close pipeline — archiveOnClose (substantive\\n   * guard + priorArchive carry-forward + LLM summary), onBeforeClose\\n   * (transcript + working-state) — so the new session's create() finds the\\n   * archive on disk and injects the handoff context into its preamble.\\n   * Throws when the backend key is unknown or a turn is in flight.\\n   */\\n  async switchAgent(chatId: number, backendKey: string, userId?: number): Promise<ChatSession> {\\n    const def = loadAcpProviders()[backendKey];\\n    if (!def) {\\n      throw new Error(`未知的 agent \\\"${backendKey}\\\"（不在 acp-providers.json）`);\\n    }\\n    const existing = this.sessions.get(chatId);\\n    if (existing?.inflight) {\\n      throw new Error(\\\"目前有 turn 進行中，請等它完成或先 /cancel 再切換\\\");\\n    }\\n    if (existing) {\\n      await this.drop(chatId, \\\"agent_switch\\\");\\n    }\\n    this.acpBackendByChat.set(chatId, backendKey);\\n    savePinnedAgents(this.acpBackendByChat).catch((err) =>\\n      console.warn(`[session ${chatId}] failed to persist pinned agent:`, err),\\n    );\\n    return this.create(chatId, userId, \\\"acp\\\");\\n  }\\n\\n  /** Start (or restart) the inactivity timer for a turn. */\\n  startInactivityTimer(session: ChatSession) {\\n    this.clearInactivityTimer(session);\\n    const ms = session._toolInFlight && config.turnToolInactivityTimeoutMs > 0\\n      ? config.turnToolInactivityTimeoutMs\\n      : config.turnInactivityTimeoutMs;\\n    if (ms <= 0) return;\\n    session._inactivityTimer = setTimeout(() => {\\n      console.warn(\\n        `[session ${session.chatId}] turn inactivity timeout (${ms}ms, toolInFlight=${!!session._toolInFlight}) — auto-cancelling`,\\n      );\\n      session.client.cancel();\\n    }, ms);\\n    session._inactivityTimer.unref?.();\\n  }\\n\\n  private resetInactivityTimer(session: ChatSession) {\\n    if (!session._inactivityTimer) return;\\n    this.startInactivityTimer(session);\\n  }\\n\\n  clearInactivityTimer(session: ChatSession) {\\n    if (session._inactivityTimer) {\\n      clearTimeout(session._inactivityTimer);\\n      session._inactivityTimer = undefined;\\n    }\\n  }\\n\\n  private applyUpdate(session: ChatSession, u: SessionUpdate) {\\n    this.resetInactivityTimer(session);\\n    const up = u.update;\\n    // Opportunistically capture usage-like meta the agent may attach.\\n    const meta = (up as { _meta?: Record<string, unknown> })._meta;\\n    const usage =\\n      (meta?.usage as Record<string, unknown> | undefined) ??\\n      ((up as { usage?: Record<string, unknown> }).usage);\\n    if (usage && typeof usage === \\\"object\\\") {\\n      session.usage = usage;\\n    }\\n    switch (up.sessionUpdate) {\\n      case \\\"agent_message_chunk\\\":\\n      case \\\"agent_thought_chunk\\\": {\\n        const content = up.content as { type?: string; text?: string } | undefined;\\n        if (content?.type === \\\"text\\\" && typeof content.text === \\\"string\\\") {\\n          // Orphan turn: a message chunk arrived with no prompt turn in-flight\\n          // (typically a background sub-agent completion re-invoking the agent\\n          // via task-notification). Thought chunks are never\\n          // user-facing, so only real message chunks take this path.\\n          if (up.sessionUpdate === \\\"agent_message_chunk\\\" && !session.inflight) {\\n            this.appendOrphan(session, content.text);\\n            break;\\n          }\\n          const wasEmpty = session.buffer.length === 0 && !session.thinkingBuffer;\\n          if (up.sessionUpdate === \\\"agent_thought_chunk\\\") {\\n            session.thinkingBuffer = (session.thinkingBuffer || \\\"\\\") + content.text;\\n          } else {\\n            session.buffer += content.text;\\n            session.charsOut += content.text.length;\\n          }\\n          if (wasEmpty) petState(\\\"streaming\\\");\\n          // Early-capture RELAY_DELEGATE tokens during streaming.\\n          // Workaround: Kiro CLI may strip <<...>> from final text in pure-text turns,\\n          // but streaming chunks arrive unfiltered. content.thinking:
The user is continuing to observe code review activity. I'm seeing multiple file reads related to security-sensitive areas:

1. **observerTransformer.ts** - Token parsing logic for <<SEND_FILE>>, <<SCHEDULE>>, <<ASK>>, and other control tokens. This shows extensive balanced-token parsing with code region masking to prevent injection attacks.

2. **Authorization patterns** - Grep searches for `ALLOWED_USER_IDS`, `isAllowed`, showing authorization checks across command handlers.

3. **File path validation** - Searches for `SEND_FILE`, `allowedRoot`, `isPathAllowed` showing path traversal protection mechanisms.

4. **status-server.ts** - HTTP server that binds to localhost by default, includes path traversal protection with `safeDecode` and whitelist validation for specialist names.

5. **telegram-ui.ts** and **relay.ts** - Both implement `isUnderAllowedRoot()` function to validate file paths against allowed roots before sending files.

Key security findings from the observed code:
- Path traversal protection via `isUnderAllowedRoot()` checking file paths against whitelist
- Percent-encoding validation in status-server with `safeDecode()` 
- Specialist name whitelist validation to prevent directory traversal
- Environment variable masking in API responses
- localhost-only binding by default for status server
- Authorization checks via `isAllowed()` wrapper

This appears to be a thorough code review focusing on security aspects (path traversal, injection, authorization).

content.text:
<observation>
  <type>discovery</type>
  <title>Security controls discovered in bridge codebase</title>
  <subtitle>Path traversal protection and authorization mechanisms validated across multiple modules</subtitle>
  <facts>
    <fact>observerTransformer.ts implements balanced-token parser with code-region masking to prevent injection from fenced/inline code blocks</fact>
    <fact>isUnderAllowedRoot() function implemented identically in telegram-ui.ts and relay.ts validates file paths against config.sessionCwd and config.sendFileRoots whitelist</fact>
    <fact>status-server.ts binds localhost (127.0.0.1) by default unless STATUS_BIND_HOST overridden, preventing LAN exposure of specialist config/preamble/facts</fact>
    <fact>safeDecode() wrapper in status-server.ts converts percent-encoding URIError to null for 400 responses, preventing unhandled exceptions</fact>
    <fact>Specialist preamble endpoint validates name against getSpecialists() whitelist before path.join to block %2E%2E%2F directory traversal</fact>
    <fact>Environment variable masking in status-server.ts obscures key/token/secret/password/credential fields before JSON response</fact>
    <fact>Command handlers consistently gate with isAllowed(ctx) check against ALLOWED_USER_IDS before executing privileged operations</fact>
  </facts>
  <narrative>Code review discovered comprehensive security controls protecting file access and API endpoints. The bridge implements defense-in-depth for file operations: SEND_FILE and RELAY_FILE tokens validate absolute paths through isUnderAllowedRoot() comparing normalized lowercase paths against whitelist, preventing directory traversal and arbitrary file disclosure. The HTTP status server (port 3847) demonstrates security-conscious design: binds loopback-only by default, wraps decodeURIComponent in safeDecode() to prevent URIError crashes from malformed input, validates specialist names against registry before constructing file paths (blocking %2E%2E%2F traversal), and masks sensitive environment variables before serialization. Token parsing in observerTransformer.ts uses code-region masking (replacing ```fenced``` and `inline` code with sentinels) before regex extraction, preventing agents from accidentally triggering control tokens when explaining protocol syntax. Authorization follows consistent pattern across command modules with isAllowed() guard checking ctx.from?.id against ALLOWED_USER_IDS whitelist.</narrative>
  <concepts>
    <concept>how-it-works</concept>
    <concept>pattern</concept>
    <concept>gotcha</concept>
  </concepts>
  <files_read>
    <file>src/observerTransformer.ts</file>
    <file>src/status-server.ts</file>
    <file>src/telegram-ui.ts</file>
    <file>src/relay.ts</file>
  </files_read>
  <files_modified></files_modified>
</observation>

If the file doesn't\\n    // exist or is corrupt we just start fresh (same as before).\\n    loadPinnedAgents().then((map) => {\\n      for (const [k, v] of map) {\\n        if (!this.acpBackendByChat.has(k)) this.acpBackendByChat.set(k, v);\\n      }\\n    }).catch(() => {});\\n  }\\n\\n  async get(chatId: number, userId?: number): Promise<ChatSession> {\\n    const existing = this.sessions.get(chatId);\\n    if (existing && !existing.client.isClosed) {\\n      existing.lastUsed = Date.now();\\n      return existing;\\n    }\\n    return this.create(chatId, userId);\\n  }\\n\\n  private async create(chatId: number, userId?: number, providerType?: ProviderType): Promise<ChatSession> {\\n    // Inject TG_ACTIVE_USER_ID so MCP children (mcp-memory) know whose\\n    // facts file to write. await deleteArchive(chatId);\\n          console.log(`[session] discarded stale archive for chat ${chatId} (age ${ageH}h > ${config.archiveMaxAgeHours}h)`);\\n        } else {\\n          archiveBlock = \\\"\\\\n\\\" + buildRestorationBlock(archive) + \\\"\\\\n\\\\n\\\";\\n          await deleteArchive(chatId);\\n          priorArchive = archive; // stash for trivial-session carry-forward on hp.lastRun.getTime() < 48 * 3600_000;\\n      if (hp && fresh && hp.items.length > 0) {\\n        dreamStateBlock =\\n          \\\"\\\\n[Pending dream suggestions — 上次 /dream 維運的 High Priority 待辦]\\\\n\\\" +\\n          hp.items.map((it) => `- ${it}`).join(\\\"\\\\n\\\") +\\n          \\\"\\\\n當使用者說「處理建議」「dream 建議」或類似語句時，即指上述項目。\\\\n\\\" +\\n          `完整三層清單（含 Watch List）在 ${loopStatePath()}。處理完某項後，更新該檔移除或標記已處理。\\\\n` +\\n          \\\"[End pending dream suggestions]\\\\n\\\\n\\\";\\n        console.log(`[session] injected ${hp.items.length} pending dream suggestion(s) for chat ${chatId}`);\\n      }\\n    } catch { /* fail-open：STATE.md 缺失或格式不符不影響 session 建立 */ }\\n\\n    const relayTaskBlock = await formatActiveRelayTasksForPreamble(chatId);\\n    const session.resumed = true;\\n      console.log(`[session ${chatId}] resumed ACP session ${session.sessionId}`);\\n    }\\n    // Registry 生命週期：flag on 時記錄本 session（bridge 整個 crash 也能恢復）\\n    if session;\\n  }\\n\\n  /** Factory: create the right Provider based on type. */\\n  private createProvider(type: ProviderType, env: Record<string, string>, systemPrompt?: string, backend?: [\\\"memory\\\", \\\"notebooklm\\\", \\\"google\\\"] : undefined,\\n    });\\n  }\\n\\n  /**\\n   * Switch the active provider for a chat. Closes the old session and\\n   * creates a new one with the specified provider type.\\n   * Returns the new session.\\n   */\\n  async switchProvider(chatId: number, type: ProviderType, userId?: number): Promise<ChatSession> {\\n    const existing = this.sessions.get(chatId);\\n    if (existing) {\\n      this.sessions.delete(chatId);\\n      await existing.client.close().catch(() => {});\\n    }\\n    return this.create(chatId, userId, type);\\n  }\\n\\n  /**\\n   * Switch the ACP backend for a chat (/agent) with conversation handoff.\\n   * Reuses the full drop() close pipeline — archiveOnClose (substantive\\n   * guard + priorArchive carry-forward + LLM summary), onBeforeClose\\n   * (transcript + working-state) — so the new session's create() finds the\\n   * archive on disk and injects the handoff context into its preamble.\\n   * Throws when the backend key is unknown or a turn is in flight.\\n   */\\n  async switchAgent(chatId: number, backendKey: string, userId?: number): Promise<ChatSession> {\\n    const def = loadAcpProviders()[backendKey];\\n    if (!def) {\\n      throw new Error(`未知的 agent \\\"${backendKey}\\\"（不在 acp-providers.json）`);\\n    }\\n    const existing = this.sessions.get(chatId);\\n    if (existing?.inflight) {\\n      throw new Error(\\\"目前有 turn 進行中，請等它完成或先 /cancel 再切換\\\");\\n    }\\n    if (existing) {\\n      await this.drop(chatId, \\\"agent_switch\\\");\\n    }\\n    this.acpBackendByChat.set(chatId, backendKey);\\n    savePinnedAgents(this.acpBackendByChat).catch((err) =>\\n      console.warn(`[session ${chatId}] failed to persist pinned agent:`, err),\\n    );\\n    return this.create(chatId, userId, \\\"acp\\\");\\n  }\\n\\n  /** Start (or restart) the inactivity timer for a turn. */\\n  startInactivityTimer(session: ChatSession) {\\n    this.clearInactivityTimer(session);\\n    const ms = session._toolInFlight && config.turnToolInactivityTimeoutMs > 0\\n      ? config.turnToolInactivityTimeoutMs\\n      : config.turnInactivityTimeoutMs;\\n    if (ms <= 0) return;\\n    session._inactivityTimer = setTimeout(() => {\\n      console.warn(\\n        `[session ${session.chatId}] turn inactivity timeout (${ms}ms, toolInFlight=${!!session._toolInFlight}) — auto-cancelling`,\\n      );\\n      session.client.cancel();\\n    }, ms);\\n    session._inactivityTimer.unref?.();\\n  }\\n\\n  private resetInactivityTimer(session: ChatSession) {\\n    if (!session._inactivityTimer) return;\\n    this.startInactivityTimer(session);\\n  }\\n\\n  clearInactivityTimer(session: ChatSession) {\\n    if (session._inactivityTimer) {\\n      clearTimeout(session._inactivityTimer);\\n      session._inactivityTimer = undefined;\\n    }\\n  }\\n\\n  private applyUpdate(session: ChatSession, u: SessionUpdate) {\\n    this.resetInactivityTimer(session);\\n    const up = u.update;\\n    // Opportunistically capture usage-like meta the agent may attach.\\n    const meta = (up as { _meta?: Record<string, unknown> })._meta;\\n    const usage =\\n      (meta?.usage as Record<string, unknown> | undefined) ??\\n      ((up as { usage?: Record<string, unknown> }).usage);\\n    if (usage && typeof usage === \\\"object\\\") {\\n      session.usage = usage;\\n    }\\n    switch (up.sessionUpdate) {\\n      case \\\"agent_message_chunk\\\":\\n      case \\\"agent_thought_chunk\\\": {\\n        const content = up.content as { type?: string; text?: string } | undefined;\\n        if (content?.type === \\\"text\\\" && typeof content.text === \\\"string\\\") {\\n          // Orphan turn: a message chunk arrived with no prompt turn in-flight\\n          // (typically a background sub-agent completion re-invoking the agent\\n          // via task-notification). <observation>
  <type>discovery</type>
  <title>Session lifecycle and resource management architecture examined</title>
  <subtitle>Complex timeout orchestration, process health monitoring, and Windows-specific cleanup patterns validated</subtitle>
  <facts>
    <fact>sessionManager.ts implements multi-tier timeout system: turn inactivity timer (config.turnInactivityTimeoutMs), tool-specific timeout (config.turnToolInactivityTimeoutMs), and orphan turn debounce (config.orphanFlushQuiescenceMs)</fact>
    <fact>Session health monitor probes subprocess liveness via process.kill(pid, 0) non-destructive check, detects silent deaths on Windows where exit event doesn't fire</fact>
    <fact>Cancel operation sets _cancelledTurn flag and _orphanSuppressUntil window (20s) to prevent trailing chunks from cancelled turn leaking to orphan path</fact>
    <fact>Process termination uses Windows-aware strategy: taskkill /T /F /PID for tree kill (acpClient.ts, pet-server.ts) vs simple kill() on POSIX</fact>
    <fact>acpClient.ts implements shell-aware spawning: .bat/.cmd/.ps1 and bare commands use shell:true with quoted command line, .exe spawns directly to avoid DEP0190 warning</fact>
    <fact>Full system PATH restored from registry via PowerShell on Windows to workaround npm run-script stripping PATH to node_modules/.bin + system32</fact>
    <fact>isAllowed() authorization function consistently gates privileged operations by checking ctx.from?.id against config.allowedUserIds whitelist</fact>
    <fact>Orphan turn flusher (sessionManager.flushOrphan) delivers agent output arriving with no active prompt turn, reusing transformReplyTokens for consistent token handling</fact>
  </facts>
  <narrative>Deep examination of session lifecycle management reveals sophisticated resource handling designed for Windows/POSIX dual platform reliability. SessionManager orchestrates three concurrent timeout mechanisms: per-turn inactivity (auto-cancel stuck prompts), tool-specific extension (longer timeout during tool execution), and orphan turn debounce (accumulate background agent output chunks before delivery). Health monitor detects subprocess silent death via non-destructive process.kill(pid, 0) probe—critical on Windows where parent shell exit doesn't reliably fire exit events. Cancel implementation demonstrates defensive programming: sets _cancelledTurn flag for run-prompt skip logic, immediately clears inflight to unblock user, establishes 20-second orphan suppression window preventing trailing chunks from cancelled turn incorrectly routing to orphan flusher. Process cleanup uses platform-aware strategy: Windows taskkill /T /F for recursive tree termination (prevents electron/tool orphans), POSIX simple kill(). ACP client spawn logic navigates Windows shell requirements: .bat/.cmd/.ps1 and extensionless commands need shell:true with manual quoting (cmd.exe primitives), .exe spawns directly avoiding DEP0190 deprecation. PATH reconstruction via PowerShell registry query compensates for npm stripping environment. Authorization layer (isAllowed) uniformly gates commands via allowedUserIds whitelist check.</narrative>

Promise.resolve();\\n  const next = prev.then(async () => {\\n    await mkdir(path.dirname(absPath), { recursive: true });\\n    const tmpPath = `${absPath}.tmp-${process.pid}-${Date.now()}`;\\n    const body = JSON.stringify(data, null, 2) + \\\"\\\\n\\\";\\n    try {\\n      await writeFile(tmpPath, body, \\\"utf8\\\");\\n      await renameWithWindowsRetry(tmpPath, absPath);\\n    } catch (err) {\\n      // Best-effort cleanup; ignore failures since tmpPath may not exist.\\n      await unlink(tmpPath).catch(() => {});\\n      throw err;\\n    }\\n  });\\n  // Attach a catch so an earlier failure doesn't poison subsequent writes:\\n  // each caller awaits its own `next`, but the chain's `prev` for future\\n  // writers should resolve either way.\\n  writePendingByPath.set(\\n    absPath,\\n    next.catch((err) => {\\n      console.error(`[atomicJson] atomicWriteJson(${absPath}) failed:`, err);\\n    }),\\n  );\\n  await next;\\n}\\n\\n/**\\n * Small convenience for code that reads, mutates, and writes back under\\n * the same serialization guarantee. The `update()` callback runs inside\\n * the write chain, so concurrent update() calls on the same path are\\n * serialized read-modify-write — no lost-update race.\\n *\\n * `initial` is used when the file does not exist or is malformed.\\n */\\nexport async function updateJson<T>(\\n  filePath: string,\\n  initial: () => T,\\n  update: (current: T) => T | Promise<T>,\\n): Promise<T> {\\n  const absPath = path.resolve(filePath);\\n  const prev = writePendingByPath.get(absPath) ?? Promise.resolve();\\n  let result: T;\\n  const next = prev.then(async () => {\\n    const current = await readJsonOrDefault<T>(absPath, initial);\\n    const updated = await update(current);\\n    result = updated;\\n    await mkdir(path.dirname(absPath), { recursive: true });\\n    const tmpPath = `${absPath}.tmp-${process.pid}-${Date.now()}`;\\n    const body = JSON.stringify(updated, null, 2) + \\\"\\\\n\\\";\\n    try {\\n      await writeFile(tmpPath, body, \\\"utf8\\\");\\n      await renameWithWindowsRetry(tmpPath, absPath);\\n    } catch (err) {\\n      await unlink(tmpPath).catch(() => {});\\n      throw err;\\n    }\\n  });\\n  writePendingByPath.set(\\n    absPath,\\n    next.catch((err) => {\\n      console.error(`[atomicJson] updateJson(${absPath}) failed:`, err);\\n    }),\\n  );\\n  await next;\\n  // Non-null assertion is safe: if `next` resolved, `result` was assigned.\\n  return result!;\\n}\\n\",\"numLines\":182,\"startLine\":1,\"totalLines\":182}}"</outcome>

  <outcome>"{\"type\":\"text\",\"file\":{\"filePath\":\"G:/AI/telegram-kiro-bridge-main/src/async-coalesce.ts\",\"content\":\"/**\\n * 非同步呼叫合併器（coalescing runner）。\\n *\\n * 把一個 async fn 包成「同一時間最多一個 in-flight」的 runner：\\n * - 進行中再被呼叫 → 標記 pending，立即返回（不排隊、不並發）\\n * - 當前執行結束後若有 pending → 補跑一次（拿到最新狀態即可，中間的呼叫合併掉）\\n * - isCancelled() 為 true → 不啟動、也不補跑\\n *\\n * 動機（2026-07-11 roadmap 事故）：streaming 的 editNow 被 onUpdate（每\\n * chunk）/ keepalive / rate-limit recovery 多路觸發，且節流時間戳只在成功\\n * 後更新——送出一旦開始失敗，時間閘門全開，並發呼叫在「draft 失敗 →\\n * !placeholder → sendMessage」的降級分支各自建立 placeholder，一口氣冒出\\n * 十幾則相同內容的訊息。latch 讓降級分支天然序列化，TOCTOU 消失。\\n */\\nexport function coalesceAsyncRunner(\\n  fn: () => Promise<void>,\\n  isCancelled: () => boolean = () => false,\\n): () => Promise<void> {\\n  let inFlight = false;\\n  let pending = false;\\n  const run = async (): Promise<void> => {\\n    if (isCancelled()) return;\\n    if (inFlight) {\\n      pending = true;\\n      return;\\n    }\\n    inFlight = true;\\n    try {\\n      await fn();\\n    } finally {\\n      inFlight = false;\\n      if (pending) {\\n        pending = false;\\n        if (!isCancelled()) void run();\\n      }\\n    }\\n  };\\n  return run;\\n}\\n\",\"numLines\":40,\"startLine\":1,\"totalLines\":40}}"</outcome>

string;\\n  progress?: number;\\n  ttl_ms?: number;\\n}\\n\\nconst PORT = Number(process.env.PET_PORT ?? \\\"38470\\\");\\nconst ENABLED = (() => {\\n  const raw = (process.env.PET_ENABLED ?? \\\"0\\\").trim().toLowerCase();\\n  return raw === \\\"1\\\" || raw === \\\"true\\\" || raw === \\\"yes\\\";\\n})();\\n\\nlet wss: any = null;\\nconst clients = new Set<any>();\\nlet idleTimer: NodeJS.Timeout | null = null;\\nconst IDLE_DELAY_MS = 5 * 60 * 1000; // 5 min no activity → idle\\n\\nexport function startPetServer(): void {\\n  if (!ENABLED || !wsAvailable) return;\\n  wss = new ws.on(\\\"error\\\", () => clients.delete(ws));\\n    ws.on(\\\"message\\\", (raw: any) => {\\n      try {\\n        const msg = JSON.parse(raw.toString());\\n        if (msg.type === \\\"chat\\\" && typeof msg.text === \\\"string\\\" && chatHandler) {\\n          chatHandler(msg.text);\\n        }\\n      } catch { /* ignore malformed */ }\\n    });\\n  });\\n  console.log(`[pet] WS server listening on ws://127.0.0.1:${PORT}`);\\n}\\n\\ntype PetChatHandler = (text: string) => void;\\nlet chatHandler: PetChatHandler | null = null;\\nexport function onPetChat(handler: PetChatHandler): void {\\n  chatHandler = handler;\\n}\\n\\nexport function petEmit(event: PetEvent): void {\\n  if (!wsAvailable || !wss || clients.size === 0) return;\\n  const msg = JSON.stringify(event);\\n  for (const ws of clients) {\\n    if (ws.readyState === WebSocket.OPEN) ws.send(msg);\\n  }\\n  resetIdleTimer();\\n}\\n\\n/** Convenience: emit a state change */\\nexport function petState(state: PetState, text?: string): void {\\n  petEmit({ type: \\\"state\\\", state, ...(text ? { text } : {}) });\\n}\\n\\nfunction resetIdleTimer(): void {\\n  if (idleTimer) clearTimeout(idleTimer);\\n  idleTimer = setTimeout(() => petState(\\\"idle\\\"), IDLE_DELAY_MS);\\n  idleTimer.unref?.();\\n}\\n\\nexport function stopPetServer(): void {\\n  if (idleTimer) clearTimeout(idleTimer);\\n  wss?.close();\\n  wss = null;\\n  clients.clear();\\n}\\n\\n// ─── Pet app process management ───────────────────────────────────────────\\nimport { spawn, execSync } from \\\"node:child_process\\\";\\nimport path from \\\"node:path\\\";\\n\\nconst PET_DIR = path.resolve(process.cwd(), \\\"desktop-pet\\\");\\nlet petProc: ReturnType<typeof spawn> | null = null;\\n\\nexport function isPetRunning(): boolean {\\n  return petProc !== null && petProc.exitCode === null;\\n}\\n\\nexport function startPetApp(): string {\\n  if (!wsAvailable) return \\\"❌ 'ws' 套件未安裝，pet 功能不可用\\\";\\n  if (isPetRunning()) return \\\"Pet 已經在跑了\\\";\\n  // Ensure WS server is up\\n  if (!wss && ENABLED) startPetServer();\\n  else if (!wss && !ENABLED && wsAvailable) {\\n    try {\\n      wss = new WebSocketServer({ port: PORT, host: \\\"127.0.0.1\\\" });\\n      wss.on(\\\"connection\\\", (ws: any) => {\\n        clients.add(ws);\\n        ws.on(\\\"close\\\", () => clients.delete(ws));\\n        ws.on(\\\"error\\\", () => clients.delete(ws));\\n        ws.on(\\\"message\\\", (raw: any) => {\\n          try {\\n            const msg = JSON.parse(raw.toString());\\n            if (msg.type === \\\"chat\\\" && typeof msg.text === \\\"string\\\" && chatHandler) {\\n              chatHandler(msg.text);\\n            }\\n          } catch { /* ignore malformed */ }\\n        });\\n      });\\n      console.log(`[pet] WS server force-started on ws://127.0.0.1:${PORT}`);\\n    } catch (e: any) {\\n      return `❌ WS server 啟動失敗: ${e.message}`;\\n    }\\n  }\\n  const electron = path.join(process.cwd(), \\\"node_modules\\\", \\\".bin\\\", \\\"electron.cmd\\\");\\n  console.log(`[pet] spawning: ${electron} in ${PET_DIR}`);\\n  petProc = spawn(electron, [\\\".\\\"], { cwd: PET_DIR, stdio: [\\\"ignore\\\", \\\"pipe\\\", \\\"pipe\\\"], detached: false, shell: true });\\n  petProc.stderr?.on(\\\"data\\\", (d: Buffer) => console.error(`[pet:err] ${d.toString().trim()}`));\\n  petProc.on(\\\"exit\\\", (code) => { console.log(`[pet] exited code=${code}`); petProc = null; });\\n  petProc.on(\\\"error\\\", (e) => console.error(`[pet] spawn error: ${e.message}`));\\n  return \\\"✅ Pet 已啟動\\\";\\n}\\n\\nexport function stopPetApp(): string {\\n  if (!isPetRunning()) return \\\"Pet 沒在跑\\\";\\n  try {\\n    execSync(`taskkill /T /F /PID ${petProc!.pid}`, { stdio: \\\"ignore\\\" });\\n  } catch { /* already dead */ }\\n  petProc = null;\\n  return \\\"✅ Pet 已關閉\\\";\\n}\\n\\nexport function petStatus(): string {\\n  const running = isPetRunning();\\n  const wsClients = clients.size;\\n  return running\\n    ? `🐾 Pet 運行中 (PID ${petProc!.pid}, ${wsClients} WS client)`\\n    : `😴 Pet 未運行 (WS server ${wss ? \\\"on\\\" : \\\"off\\\"}, ${wsClients} client)`;\\n}\\n\",\"numLines\":155,\"startLine\":1,\"totalLines\":155}}"</outcome>

<observation>
  <type>bugfix</type>
  <title>Concurrency bug fixed with async coalescing pattern</title>
  <subtitle>Streaming edit concurrency caused duplicate placeholder messages, resolved with call coalescing runner</subtitle>
  <facts>
    <fact>async-coalesce.ts implements coalescing runner pattern ensuring at most one in-flight async call at a time</fact>
    <fact>2026-07-11 roadmap incident: streaming editNow triggered by onUpdate/keepalive/recovery simultaneously created multiple identical placeholder messages</fact>
    <fact>Root cause was TOCTOU race in draft-to-placeholder fallback path where !placeholder guard evaluated true concurrently during sendMessage降級</fact>
    <fact>Coalescing logic marks pending=true during in-flight execution, consolidates intermediate calls into single补跑 after current execution completes</fact>
    <fact>run-prompt.ts applies coalesceAsyncRunner to editNowInner with turnFinalized cancellation predicate</fact>
  </facts>
  <narrative>Code review discovered async-coalesce.ts module implementing concurrency control pattern that fixed a production incident. The 2026-07-11 roadmap bug manifested as dozens of duplicate placeholder messages flooding the chat during streaming turns. Analysis traced the issue to editNow being triggered from multiple sources (onUpdate per chunk, draft keepalive timer, rate-limit recovery callback) without mutual exclusion. When draft streaming failed and fell back to placeholder creation, the time-based throttle (lastEditAt) only updated after successful send—during failure windows, the gate stayed open and concurrent callers all evaluated !placeholder as true, each spawning bot.api.sendMessage. The coalescing runner wraps editNowInner ensuring serialization: if called while in-flight, it sets pending flag and returns immediately (no queueing); after current execution, it runs exactly once more if pending was set, consolidating all intermediate triggers. The isCancelled predicate (turnFinalized) prevents post-finalization runs. This pattern eliminates TOCTOU races in the fallback path without heavyweight locking.</narrative>
  <concepts>
    <concept>problem-solution</concept>
    <concept>pattern</concept>
    <concept>gotcha</concept>
  </concepts>
  <files_read>
    <file>src/async-coalesce.ts</file>
    <file>src/run-prompt.ts</file>
  </files_read>
  <files_modified></files_modified>
</observation>

<observation>
  <type>discovery</type>
  <title>Telegram Bot Token isolation prevents subprocess conflicts</title>
  <subtitle>buildSpawnEnv explicitly blanks TELEGRAM_BOT_TOKEN to disable Telegram plugin in spawned agents</subtitle>
  <facts>
    <fact>buildSpawnEnv in acpClient.ts sets base.TELEGRAM_BOT_TOKEN to empty string before spawning child processes</fact>
    <fact>Telegram API allows only one getUpdates consumer per bot token at a time</fact>
    <fact>Claude Code Telegram plugin reads token from environment variables with priority over config file</fact>
    <fact>Empty string environment variable prevents plugin fallback to config file, causing self-disable rather than conflict</fact>
    <fact>Design prevents 409 Conflict errors that would kill bridge's main polling loop when spawned headless sessions attempt concurrent getUpdates</fact>
  </facts>
  <narrative>Discovered security-conscious environment isolation in subprocess spawning logic. The bridge clears TELEGRAM_BOT_TOKEN before spawning ACP agent subprocesses to prevent Telegram API conflicts. If the parent bridge's bot token leaked into child environment, Claude Code's Telegram plugin (which prioritizes env vars over settings files) would use the same token for getUpdates polling. Telegram's API constraint allows only one simultaneous getUpdates consumer per token, so concurrent polling attempts return 409 Conflict and terminate the connection—killing the bridge's own message loop. Setting token to empty string (rather than delete) exploits plugin behavior: when it sees "defined but empty", it treats that as explicit disable directive rather than falling back to read ~/.claude/telegram.json. This ensures headless spawned sessions never open their own Telegram channel, maintaining the bridge as the sole consumer.</narrative>
  <concepts>
    <concept>how-it-works</concept>
    <concept>gotcha</concept>
    <concept>pattern</concept>
  </concepts>
  <files_read>
    <file>src/acpClient.ts</file>
  </files_read>
  <files_modified></files_modified>
</observation>

<observation>
  <type>discovery</type>
  <title>ASK token state tracked in-memory registry keyed by message ID</title>
  <subtitle>askRegistry maps Telegram message_id to ASK button definitions for callback routing</subtitle>
  <facts>
    <fact>askRegistry defined in telegram-ui.ts as Map<number, AskEntry[]> keyed by Telegram message_id</fact>
    <fact>Populated when messages with ASK keyboards are sent: placeholder edits, final renders, specialist proxy replies</fact>
    <fact>Callback query handler in index.ts reads askRegistry to resolve button key to question_id and original options</fact>
    <fact>Registry entries deleted after user clicks button to prevent stale callbacks on old messages</fact>
    <fact>No persistence layer: registry resets on bridge restart, orphaning pending ASK keyboards until message expires</fact>
    <fact>Usage sites include run-prompt.ts (main path), commands/relay.ts (relay path), index.ts (specialist proxy)</fact>
  </facts>
  <narrative>Code review mapped the lifecycle of ASK interactive button state management. The bridge maintains an in-memory registry mapping Telegram message_id (the unique identifier Telegram assigns when a message is posted) to parsed ASK token definitions. When the agent emits <<ASK:question_id|key=label>> tokens, transformReplyTokens extracts them into AskEntry structures, buildAskKeyboard renders them as InlineKeyboard markup, and the registry records the association for callback routing. When a user clicks a button, Telegram sends a callback_query with the original message_id and the button's callback_data. The handler retrieves the AskEntry[] from askRegistry, matches the button key, and can inject the user's choice as a follow-up prompt. Entries are deleted after use to prevent double-clicks or stale interactions on old messages. The registry is ephemeral—no disk persistence—so bridge restarts orphan any pending ASK keyboards (the buttons remain visible but non-functional until Telegram's 48-hour inline keyboard TTL expires). Multiple code paths populate the registry: main reply flow, relay headless delivery, and specialist proxy messages.</narrative>
  <concepts>
    <concept>how-it-works</concept>
    <concept>gotcha</concept>
  </concepts>

    <file>src/commands/relay.ts</file>
  </files_read>
  <files_modified></files_modified>
</observation>

Schedules that far out get re-armed in chunks.\\n */\\n\\nconst MAX_TIMEOUT_MS = 2_147_483_647; // (2^31 - 1) ≈ 24.85 days\\n\\nexport interface SchedulerOptions {\\n  /** Called when a schedule finishes (success or failure). */\\n  onFire?: (entry: ScheduleEntry, result: FireResult) => void;\\n}\\n\\nexport interface FireResult {\\n  ok: boolean;\\n  replyChars: number;\\n  /** Present when the agent prompt threw or timed out. */\\n  error?: string;\\n}\\n\\nexport class Scheduler {\\n  private timers = new Map<string, NodeJS.Timeout>();\\n  private commandHandlers: BridgeCommandHandlers = {};\\n\\n  constructor(\\n    private readonly bot: Bot,\\n    private readonly store: ScheduleStore,\\n    private readonly options: SchedulerOptions = {},\\n  ) {}\\n\\n  /**\\n   * Register bridge command handlers (COMMAND_HANDLERS from index.ts). Idempotent per id. */\\n  arm(entry: ScheduleEntry): void {\\n    this.disarm(entry.id);\\n    // Defensive: a non-finite runAt (NaN / Infinity / null coerced in from a\\n    // corrupted schedules.json entry) would become setTimeout(NaN) — Node\\n    // clamps that to ~1ms and logs TimeoutNaNWarning, causing the scheduler\\n    // to hot-loop firing the same entry forever. Drop the entry instead.\\n    if (!Number.isFinite(entry.runAt)) {\\n      console.error(\\n        `[scheduler] refusing to arm ${entry.id}: runAt is not finite (${entry.runAt}); dropping entry to avoid hot-loop`,\\n      );\\n      this.store.forgetInMemory(entry.id);\\n      // Fire-and-forget the persist — we can't await here because arm() is\\n      // called synchronously from many places (armAll, post-fire re-arm,\\n      // /schedule add).\\n      this.store.persist().catch((err) =>\\n        console.error(`[scheduler] persist after dropping ${entry.id} failed:`, err),\\n      );\\n      // Notify the user so a silently-dropped recurring schedule doesn't\\n      // surprise them on next /schedule list.\\n      const msg =\\n        `⚠️ 排程 ${entry.id} 已被移除：runAt 為非有限值 (${entry.runAt})，可能是 recurrence 缺欄位或舊版資料殘留。\\\\n` +\\n        `prompt: ${entry.prompt.length > 80 ? Does not touch the store. */\\n  disarm(id: string): void {\\n    const t = this.timers.get(id);\\n    if (t) {\\n      clearTimeout(t);\\n      this.timers.delete(id);\\n    }\\n  }\\n\\n  /** Clear all pending timers (bridge shutdown). */\\n  stop(): void {\\n    for (const t of this.timers.values()) clearTimeout(t);\\n    this.timers.clear();\\n  }\\n\\n  /**\\n   * Run an entry immediately (also invoked by /schedule run). For one-shot\\n   * entries this removes the entry first so a crash during the prompt won't\\n   * duplicate on restart. For recurring entries the entry stays in storage\\n   * and gets re-armed with an updated runAt after the prompt finishes.\\n   */\\n  async fire(entry: ScheduleEntry): Promise<FireResult> {\\n    // ── Quiet Hours gate ──────────────────────────────────────────────────\\n    // If current local time falls within QUIET_HOURS, delay until the end.\\n    const qh = config.quietHours;\\n    if (qh) {\\n      const now = new Date();\\n      const h = now.getHours();\\n      const inQuiet = qh.start < qh.end\\n        ? h >= qh.start && h < qh.end        // e.g. 00-08\\n        : h >= qh.start || h < qh.end;       // e.g. 22-06 (wraps midnight)\\n      if (inQuiet) {\\n        // Compute next wakeup: today (or tomorrow) at qh.end:00:00\\n        const wake = new Date(now);\\n        wake.setHours(qh.end, 0, 0, 0);\\n        if (wake.getTime() <= now.getTime()) {\\n          wake.setDate(wake.getDate() + 1);\\n        }\\n        console.log(\\n          `[scheduler ${entry.id}] quiet hours (${qh.start}:00–${qh.end}:00) — delaying to ${wake.toLocaleTimeString()}`,\\n        );\\n        // Update runAt and re-arm\\n        entry.runAt = wake.getTime();\\n        await this.store.persist().catch(() => {});\\n        this.arm(entry);\\n        return { ok: true, replyChars: 0 };\\n      }\\n    }\\n    // ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────\\n\\n    const isRecurring = !!entry.recurrence;\\n    logEvent(\\\"scheduler_fire\\\", { \\n      entryId: entry.id, \\n      isRecurring, \\n      promptLength: entry.prompt.length \\n    }, { chatId: entry.chatId, userId: entry.userId });\\n    \\n    if (!isRecurring) {\\n      // One-shot: remove-then-run so a crash during prompt doesn't duplicate.\\n      this.store.forgetInMemory(entry.id);\\n      await this.store.persist().catch((err) =>\\n        console.error(`[scheduler] persist after fire ${entry.id} failed:`, err),\\n      );\\n    }\\n\\n    const tag = `[scheduler ${entry.id}]`;\\n    console.log(\\n      `${tag} firing (chat=${entry.chatId} user=${entry.userId} promptChars=${entry.prompt.length})`,\\n    );\\n\\n    // Try bridge command dispatch first: \\\"/restart\\\", \\\"/schedule list\\\", etc.\\n    // Handlers that call process.exit (e.g. /restart) will terminate the\\n    // process before we reach the recurring re-arm block — that's fine;\\n    // recurring entries are already persisted in the store and will be\\n    // re-armed on the next startup.\\n    //\\n    // Messaging model: we post a \\\"▶️ start\\\" marker BEFORE the handler runs\\n    // and an \\\"✅ done\\\" / \\\"❌ failed\\\" marker AFTER. The handler itself is\\n    // free to ctx.reply()/editMessageText() live (needed for runPrompt\\n    // streaming in /dream steps like /memorytoskill, /topicreview, etc.\\n    // — they need a real message_id). This means the old \\\"single grouped\\n    // reply under one header\\\" UX is gone; in return, long tasks actually\\n    // show their output instead of silently dropping every edit.\\n    const recurHint = entry.recurrence\\n      ? `（${formatRecurrence(entry.recurrence)}）`\\n      : \\\"\\\";\\n    try {\\n      await this.bot.api.sendMessage(\\n        entry.chatId,\\n        `⏰ 排程 ${entry.id}${recurHint}（指令）開始執行…`,\\n      );\\n    } catch (err) {\\n      console.error(`${tag} start marker sendMessage failed:`, err);\\n    }\\n\\n    const bridgeRes = await tryRunBridgeCommand(\\n      this.bot,\\n      entry,\\n      this.commandHandlers,\\n    );\\n    let result: FireResult = { ok: false, replyChars: 0 };\\n    if (bridgeRes.handled) {\\n      const ok = bridgeRes.error === undefined;\\n      const tail = ok\\n        ? `✅ 排程 ${entry.id}${recurHint}（指令）執行完成` +\\n          (bridgeRes.messagesSent === 0 && bridgeRes.filesSent === 0\\n            ? \\\"\\\\n(handler 沒有產出文字或檔案)\\\"\\n            : bridgeRes.filesSent > 0\\n              ? `\\\\n(傳了 ${bridgeRes.filesSent} 個檔案)`\\n              : \\\"\\\")\\n        : `❌ 排程 ${entry.id}${recurHint}（指令）執行失敗\\\\n錯誤：${bridgeRes.error}`;\\n      const text = truncate(tail, 3800);\\n      try {\\n        await this.bot.api.sendMessage(entry.chatId, text);\\n      } catch (err) {\\n        console.error(`${tag} end marker sendMessage failed:`, err);\\n      }\\n      result = {\\n        ok,\\n        replyChars: 0,\\n        error: bridgeRes.error,\\n      };\\n      // Jump to recurring re-arm tail.\\n      return this.afterFire(entry, result, tag);\\n    }\\n\\n    const env: Record<string, string> = {\\n      MEMORY_DIR: config.memoryDir,\\n      TG_ACTIVE_USER_ID: String(entry.userId),\\n    };\\n    const client = new AcpClient({\\n      command: config.agent.command,\\n      args: config.agent.args,\\n      cwd: config.sessionCwd,\\n      env,\\n      promptRetries: config.acpPromptRetries,\\n      promptRetryBaseMs: config.acpPromptRetryBaseMs,\\n    });\\n    let buffer = \\\"\\\";\\n    client.on(\\\"update\\\", (u) => {\\n      const up = u.update;\\n      if (\\n        up.sessionUpdate === \\\"agent_message_chunk\\\" ||\\n        up.sessionUpdate === \\\"agent_thought_chunk\\\"\\n      ) {\\n        const content = up.content as { type?: string; text?: string } | undefined;\\n        if (content?.type === \\\"text\\\" && typeof content.text === \\\"string\\\") {\\n          buffer += content.text;\\n        }\\n      }\\n    });\\n\\n    try {\\n      await client.initializeAndCreateSession(config.sessionCwd);\\n      // Inject long-term memory so the agent has user context.\\n      const facts = entry.userId ? Truncate long replies to fit a single\\n    // message — scheduled output is \\\"fire and forget\\\", no streaming UI.\\n    const header = `⏰ 排程 ${entry.id}${recurHint} 執行${result.ok ? \\\"完成\\\" : \\\"失敗\\\"}`;\\n    const body = result.ok\\n      ? buffer.trim() || \\\"(agent 沒有產出回覆)\\\"\\n      : `錯誤：${result.error ?? \\\"unknown\\\"}`;\\n    const text = truncate(`${header}\\\\n\\\\n${body}`, 3800);\\n    try {\\n      await this.bot.api.sendMessage(entry.chatId, text);\\n    } catch (err) {\\n      console.error(`${tag} sendMessage to ${entry.chatId} failed:`, err);\\n    }\\n\\n    return this.afterFire(entry, result, tag);\\n  }\\n\\n  /**\\n   * Shared tail for fire(): recurring re-arm + onFire callback.   <outcome>"{\"type\":\"text\",\"file\":{\"filePath\":\"G:\\\\AI\\\\telegram-kiro-bridge-main\\\\src\\\\async-coalesce.ts\",\"content\":\"/**\\n * 非同步呼叫合併器（coalescing runner）。\\n *\\n * 把一個 async fn 包成「同一時間最多一個 in-flight」的 runner：\\n * - 進行中再被呼叫 → 標記 pending，立即返回（不排隊、不並發）\\n * - 當前執行結束後若有 pending → 補跑一次（拿到最新狀態即可，中間的呼叫合併掉）\\n * - isCancelled() 為 true → 不啟動、也不補跑\\n *\\n * 動機（2026-07-11 roadmap 事故）：streaming 的 editNow 被 onUpdate（每\\n * chunk）/ keepalive / rate-limit recovery 多路觸發，且節流時間戳只在成功\\n * 後更新——送出一旦開始失敗，時間閘門全開，並發呼叫在「draft 失敗 →\\n * !placeholder → sendMessage」的降級分支各自建立 placeholder，一口氣冒出\\n * 十幾則相同內容的訊息。latch 讓降級分支天然序列化，TOCTOU 消失。\\n */\\nexport function coalesceAsyncRunner(\\n  fn: () => Promise<void>,\\n  isCancelled: () => boolean = () => false,\\n): () => Promise<void> {\\n  let inFlight = false;\\n  let pending = false;\\n  const run = async (): Promise<void> => {\\n    if (isCancelled()) return;\\n    if (inFlight) {\\n      pending = true;\\n      return;\\n    }\\n    inFlight = true;\\n    try {\\n      await fn();\\n    } finally {\\n      inFlight = false;\\n      if (pending) {\\n        pending = false;\\n        if (!isCancelled()) void run();\\n      }\\n    }\\n  };\\n  return run;\\n}\\n\",\"numLines\":40,\"startLine\":1,\"totalLines\":40}}"</outcome>

  <outcome>"{\"type\":\"text\",\"file\":{\"filePath\":\"G:/AI/telegram-kiro-bridge-main/src/local-llm.ts\",\"content\":\" */\\nlet _llamaCppBusy = false;\\nexport function setLlamaCppBusy(busy: boolean): void { _llamaCppBusy = busy; }\\nexport function isLlamaCppBusy(): boolean { return _llamaCppBusy; }\\n\\n/** Check if llama.cpp server is reachable. Caches success permanently; retries failure after interval. */\\nexport async function detectLlamaCpp(): Promise<boolean> {\\n  if (_available === true) return true;\\n  if (_available === false && Date.now() - _lastFailAt < RETRY_INTERVAL_MS) return false;\\n  try {\\n    const res = await fetch(`${LLAMACPP_BASE}/health`, { signal: AbortSignal.timeout(3000) });\\n    if (!res.ok) { _available = false; _lastFailAt = Date.now(); return false; }\\n    _available = true;\\n    console.log(`[local-llm] llama.cpp server detected at ${LLAMACPP_BASE}`);\\n    return true;\\n  } catch {\\n    _available = false;\\n    _lastFailAt = Date.now();\\n    return false;\\n  }\\n}\\n\\n/** @deprecated Use detectLlamaCpp(). Kept for backward compat during migration. */\\nexport const detectOllama = detectLlamaCpp;\\n\\n/**\\n * Auto-start llama.cpp server if LLAMACPP_START_CMD is set and server isn't running.\\n * Called once at bridge startup. Spawns detached so it survives bridge restarts.\\n * Returns true if server became available (either already running or successfully started).\\n */\\nexport async function autoStartLlamaCpp(): Promise<boolean> {\\n  if (await detectLlamaCpp()) return true;\\n  if (!LLAMACPP_START_CMD) return false;\\n\\n  console.log(`[local-llm] server not running, launching: ${LLAMACPP_START_CMD}`);\\n  try {\\n    const cwd = path.dirname(LLAMACPP_START_CMD);\\n    _serverProc = spawn(\\\"cmd.exe\\\", [\\\"/c\\\", \\\"start\\\", \\\"/min\\\", \\\"\\\", LLAMACPP_START_CMD], {\\n      cwd,\\n      detached: true,\\n      stdio: \\\"ignore\\\",\\n      windowsHide: false,\\n    });\\n    _serverProc.unref();\\n\\n    // Poll for readiness (up to 30s)\\n    for (let i = 0; i < 30; i++) {\\n      await new Promise((r) => setTimeout(r, 1000));\\n      _available = null; // reset cache\\n      if (await detectLlamaCpp()) {\\n        console.log(`[local-llm] server ready after ${i + 1}s`);\\n        return true;\\n      }\\n    }\\n    console.warn(\\\"[local-llm] server did not become ready within 30s — continuing without\\\");\\n    return false;\\n  } catch (err) {\\n    console.warn(\\\"[local-llm] failed to launch server:\\\", err);\\n    return false;\\n  }\\n}\\n\\n/** Get the model name (from env or default). */\\nexport function getModel(): string | null {\\n  return _available ? (process.env.OLLAMA_CHAT_MODEL ?? \\\"qwen3.6:35b-a3b\\\") : null;\\n}\\n\\n/** Reset detection cache (for testing or after server restart). */\\nexport function resetDetection(): void { _available = null; }\\n\\nexport interface CompleteOpts {\\n  temperature?: number;\\n  maxTokens?: number;\\n  /** Timeout in ms. Default 15000. */\\n  timeout?: number;\\n}\\n\\n/**\\n * Send a prompt to llama.cpp server and return the response text.\\n * Uses /v1/chat/completions (non-streaming). The model may use thinking tokens\",\"numLines\":80,\"startLine\":20,\"totalLines\":568}}"</outcome>

  <outcome>"{\"type\":\"text\",\"file\":{\"filePath\":\"G:\\\\AI\\\\telegram-kiro-bridge-main\\\\src\\\\relay.ts\",\"content\":\"/**\\n * Bot-to-Bot Relay — dual transport: Upstash Redis + Telegram bot-to-bot.\\n *\\n * Architecture:\\n *   sendRelay(msg) -> transport switch:\\n *     \\\"redis\\\"    -> LPUSH to Redis list \\\"relay:<targetBot>\\\"\\n *     \\\"telegram\\\" -> bot.api.sendMessage(@targetBotUsername, text)\\n *     \\\"both\\\"     -> telegram first, redis as fallback\\n *   + sendMessage to group (mirror)\\n *\\n *   Incoming:\\n *     Redis poller -> RPOP from \\\"relay:<myName>\\\" -> inject handler\\n *     Bot handler  -> from.is_bot + allowedBotIds -> inject handler\\n *\\n * Redis-only incoming relay messages are mirrored by commands/relay.ts after\\n * transport de-duplication. Telegram relay uses sender-side group mirrors so\\n * each conversational message appears once in the shared group.\\n */\\nimport { stat } from \\\"node:fs/promises\\\";\\nimport path from \\\"node:path\\\";\\nimport { InputFile, type Bot } from \\\"grammy\\\";\\nimport { config } from \\\"./config.js\\\";\\n\\nexport type RelayTransport = \\\"redis\\\" | \\\"telegram\\\" | \\\"both\\\";\\n\\nexport interface RelayConfig {\\n  redisUrl: string;\\n  redisToken: string;\\n  groupChatId: number;\\n  botName: string;\\n  transport: RelayTransport;\\n  targetBotUsername: string; // e.g. \\\"@RK_AI_Codex_bot\\\" for telegram transport\\n  maxConsecutiveTurns: number;\\n}\\n\\ninterface RelayMessage {\\n  from: string;\\n  text: string;\\n  ts: string;\\n}\\n\\nlet cfg: RelayConfig | null = null;\\nlet bot: Bot | null = null;\\nlet pollTimer: ReturnType<typeof setInterval> | null = null;\\nlet heartbeatTimer: ReturnType<typeof setInterval> | null = null;\\nlet onIncoming: ((msg: RelayMessage) => void) | null = null;\\nlet idleCount = 0;\\nconst IDLE_MAX = 40; // ~2 min at 3s interval -> auto-stop\\nconst HEARTBEAT_MS = 30_000; // low-freq check for unsolicited incoming\\nconst IMG_EXT = new Set([\\\".jpg\\\", \\\".jpeg\\\", \\\".png\\\", \\\".gif\\\", \\\".webp\\\", \\\".bmp\\\"]);\\nconst PHOTO_MAX_BYTES = 10 * 1024 * 1024;\\n\\nexport interface RelayRuntimeStatus {\\n  configured: boolean;\\n  botName: string | null;\\n  transport: RelayTransport | null;\\n  transportLabel: string;\\n  groupChatId: number | null;\\n  targetBotUsername: string | null;\\n  redisConfigured: boolean;\\n  telegramConfigured: boolean;\\n  redisPollerExpected: boolean;\\n  redisPollerActive: boolean;\\n  heartbeatActive: boolean;\\n  maxConsecutiveTurns: number | null;\\n}\\n\\nexport function describeRelayTransport(transport: RelayTransport | null): string {\\n  if (transport === \\\"redis\\\") return \\\"redis only\\\";\\n  if (transport === \\\"telegram\\\") return \\\"telegram only\\\";\\n  if (transport === \\\"both\\\") return \\\"telegram primary + redis fallback\\\";\\n  return \\\"not configured\\\";\\n}\\n\\nexport function getRelayRuntimeStatus(): RelayRuntimeStatus {\\n  if (!cfg) {\\n    return {\\n      configured: false,\\n      botName: null,\\n      transport: null,\\n      transportLabel: describeRelayTransport(null),\\n      groupChatId: null,\\n      targetBotUsername: null,\\n      redisConfigured: false,\\n      telegramConfigured: false,\\n      redisPollerExpected: false,\\n      redisPollerActive: false,\\n      heartbeatActive: false,\\n      maxConsecutiveTurns: null,\\n    };\\n  }\\n  const redisConfigured = !!(cfg.redisUrl && cfg.redisToken);\\n  const telegramConfigured = !!cfg.targetBotUsername;\\n  return {\\n    configured: true,\\n    botName: cfg.botName,\\n    transport: cfg.transport,\\n    transportLabel: describeRelayTransport(cfg.transport),\\n    groupChatId: cfg.groupChatId,\\n    targetBotUsername: cfg.targetBotUsername || null,\\n    redisConfigured,\\n    telegramConfigured,\\n    redisPollerExpected: cfg.transport !== \\\"telegram\\\" && redisConfigured,\\n    redisPollerActive: !!pollTimer,\\n    heartbeatActive: !!heartbeatTimer,\\n    maxConsecutiveTurns: cfg.maxConsecutiveTurns,\\n  };\\n}\\n\\nfunction redisKey(target: string): string {\\n  return `relay:${target}`;\\n}\\n\\nfunction isUnderAllowedRoot(abs: string): boolean {\\n  const roots = [config.sessionCwd, ...config.sendFileRoots];\\n  const normAbs = path.resolve(abs).toLowerCase();\\n  return roots.some((root) => {\\n    const r = path.resolve(root).toLowerCase().replace(/[\\\\\\\\/]+$/, \\\"\\\");\\n    if (normAbs === r) return true;\\n    return normAbs.startsWith(r + \\\"\\\\\\\\\\\") || normAbs.startsWith(r + \\\"/\\\");\\n  });\\n}\\n\\nasync function redisCommand(command: string[]): Promise<{ result: unknown }> {\\n  if (!cfg) throw new Error(\\\"Relay not initialized\\\");\\n  const res = await fetch(cfg.redisUrl, {\\n    method: \\\"POST\\\",\\n    headers: {\\n      Authorization: `Bearer ${cfg.redisToken}`,\\n      \\\"Content-Type\\\": \\\"application/json\\\",\\n    },\\n    body: JSON.stringify(command),\\n  });\\n  if (!res.ok) {\\n    const body = await res.text();\\n    throw new Error(`Redis ${command[0]} failed: ${res.status} ${body}`);\\n  }\\n  return res.json() as Promise<{ result: unknown }>;\\n}\\n\\n/** Start polling if not already running. */\\nexport function ensurePollerRunning(): void {\\n  if (pollTimer || !cfg) return;\\n  idleCount = 0;\\n  pollTimer = setInterval(poll, 3000);\\n  console.log(`[relay] poller activated`);\\n}\\n\\n/** Send a message to another bot via configured transport + mirror to group. */\\nexport async function sendRelay(targetBot: string, text: string): Promise<void> {\\n  if (!cfg || !bot) return;\\n  if (process.env.RELAY_PAUSED === \\\"true\\\") {\\n    console.log(`[relay] RELAY_PAUSED — blocked outgoing to \\\"${targetBot}\\\"`);\\n    return;\\n  }\\n  // Guard: never relay to self (agent confused the target).\\n  if (targetBot === cfg.botName) {\\n    console.warn(`[relay] blocked self-send (target=\\\"${targetBot}\\\"), text: ${text.slice(0, 80)}`);\\n    return;\\n  }\\n  // Guard: skip empty or placeholder-only messages (e.g. \\\"...\\\", \\\"…\\\").\\n  const trimmed = text.replace(/[.\\\\s…]+/g, \\\"\\\");\\n  if (!trimmed) {\\n    console.warn(`[relay] blocked empty/placeholder relay to \\\"${targetBot}\\\": \\\"${text.slice(0, 40)}\\\"`);\\n    return;\\n  }\\n\\n  const transport = cfg.transport;\\n  let sent = false;\\n\\n  // Telegram bot-to-bot transport\\n  if (transport === \\\"telegram\\\" || transport === \\\"both\\\") {\\n    const target = cfg.targetBotUsername;\\n    if (target) {\\n      try {\\n        const payload = `[RELAY:${cfg.botName}] ${text}`;\\n        await bot.api.sendMessage(target, payload);\\n        sent = true;\\n        console.log(`[relay] ✓ sent via TELEGRAM to ${target} (transport=${transport})`);\\n      } catch (err) {\\n        console.error(`[relay] telegram sendMessage to ${target} failed:`, err);\\n        if (transport === \\\"both\\\") console.log(`[relay] falling back to REDIS...`);\\n      }\\n    }\\n  }\\n\\n  // Redis transport (primary or fallback)\\n  if (transport === \\\"redis\\\" || (transport === \\\"both\\\" && !sent)) {\\n    if (cfg.redisUrl && cfg.redisToken) {\\n      const msg: RelayMessage = { from: cfg.botName, text, ts: new Date().toISOString() };\\n      const key = redisKey(targetBot);\\n      await redisCommand([\\\"LPUSH\\\", key, JSON.stringify(msg)]);\\n      sent = true;\\n        console.log(`[relay] ✓ sent via REDIS to key \\\"${key}\\\" (transport=${transport})`);\\n    }\\n  }\\n\\n  if (!sent) {\\n    console.error(`[relay] failed to send via any transport (transport=${transport})`);\\n    return;\\n  }\\n\\n  if (transport === \\\"redis\\\" || transport === \\\"both\\\") ensurePollerRunning();\\n\\n  const display = `📤 sent by ${cfg.botName} [${cfg.botName} -> ${targetBot}]\\\\n${text}`;\\n  await bot.api.sendMessage(cfg.groupChatId, display).catch((err) =>\\n    console.error(\\\"[relay] group sendMessage failed:\\\", err),\\n  );\\n}\\n\\n/** Send a file to another bot via Telegram bot-to-bot (telegram/both transport only). */\\nexport async function sendRelayFile(targetBot: string, filePath: string, caption: string = \\\"[RELAY_INPUT]\\\"): Promise<boolean> {\\n  if (!cfg || !bot) return false;\\n  if (process.env.RELAY_PAUSED === \\\"true\\\") {\\n    console.log(`[relay] RELAY_PAUSED — blocked outgoing file to \\\"${targetBot}\\\"`);\\n    return false;\\n  }\\n  if (targetBot === cfg.botName) {\\n    console.warn(`[relay] blocked self-send file (target=\\\"${targetBot}\\\")`);\\n    return false;\\n  }\\n  const abs = path.resolve(filePath);\\n  if (!isUnderAllowedRoot(abs)) {\\n    console.warn(`[relay] blocked file outside allowed roots to \\\"${targetBot}\\\": ${abs}`);\\n    return false;\\n  }\\n  let st;\\n  try {\\n    st = await stat(abs);\\n  } catch (err) {\\n    console.warn(`[relay] sendRelayFile stat failed for ${abs}:`, (err as Error).message);\\n    return false;\\n  }\\n  if (!st.isFile()) {\\n    console.warn(`[relay] blocked non-file relay attachment to \\\"${targetBot}\\\": ${abs}`);\\n    return false;\\n  }\\n  const transport = cfg.transport;\\n  if (transport === \\\"redis\\\") {\\n    console.warn(`[relay] sendRelayFile requires telegram transport, current=${transport}`);\\n    return false;\\n  }\\n  const target = cfg.targetBotUsername;\\n  if (!target) {\\n    console.warn(`[relay] sendRelayFile: no targetBotUsername configured`);\\n    return false;\\n  }\\n  try {\\n    const ext = path.extname(abs).toLowerCase();\\n    // RELAY_OUTPUT with PNG: send as document to preserve transparency.\\n    // RELAY_INPUT: send as photo (compression OK for reference images).\\n    const isOutput = caption === \\\"[RELAY_OUTPUT]\\\";\\n    const asPhoto = IMG_EXT.has(ext) && st.size <= PHOTO_MAX_BYTES && !(isOutput && ext === \\\".png\\\");\\n    if (asPhoto) {\\n      await bot.api.sendPhoto(target, new InputFile(abs), { caption });\\n    } else {\\n      await bot.api.sendDocument(target, new InputFile(abs), { caption });\\n    }\\n    console.log(`[relay] ✓ sent file via TELEGRAM to ${target}: ${abs}`);\\n    await bot.api.sendMessage(cfg.groupChatId, `📤🖼️ [${cfg.botName} -> ${targetBot}] file: ${abs}`).catch(() => {});\\n    return true;\\n  } catch (err) {\\n    console.error(`[relay] sendRelayFile to ${target} failed:`, err);\\n    return false;\\n  }\\n}\\n\\n/** Poll Redis for incoming messages. */\\nasync function poll(): Promise<void> {\\n  if (!cfg || !bot) return;\\n  const key = redisKey(cfg.botName);\\n  try {\\n    const { result } = await redisCommand([\\\"RPOP\\\", key]);\\n    if (result === null || typeof result !== \\\"string\\\") {\\n      idleCount++;\\n      if (idleCount >= IDLE_MAX && pollTimer) {\\n        clearInterval(pollTimer);\\n        pollTimer = null;\\n        console.log(`[relay] high-freq poller stopped after ${IDLE_MAX} idle cycles`);\\n      }\\n      return;\\n    }\\n    idleCount = 0;\\n    ensurePollerRunning();\\n    const msg: RelayMessage = JSON.parse(result);\\n    // Dedup: skip if already received via telegram bot-to-bot\\n    if (isDuplicate(msg.from, msg.text)) {\\n      console.log(`[relay] dedup: skipping redis message from \\\"${msg.from}\\\"`);\\n      return;\\n    }\\n    console.log(`[relay] ✓ received via REDIS from \\\"${msg.from}\\\"`);\\n    if (onIncoming) onIncoming(msg);\\n  } catch (err) {\\n    console.error(\\\"[relay] poll error:\\\", err);\\n  }\\n}\\n\\n/** Initialize relay (registers config + handler). Poller activates on demand. */\\nexport function startRelayPoller(\\n  botInstance: Bot,\\n  relayConfig: RelayConfig,\\n  handler: (msg: RelayMessage) => void,\\n): void {\\n  cfg = relayConfig;\\n  bot = botInstance;\\n  onIncoming = handler;\\n  if (pollTimer) { clearInterval(pollTimer); pollTimer = null; }\\n  if (heartbeatTimer) { clearInterval(heartbeatTimer); heartbeatTimer = null; }\\n  // Skip Redis polling entirely when transport is telegram-only.\\n  if (relayConfig.transport === \\\"telegram\\\") {\\n    console.log(`[relay] ready - // 5 分鐘\\n\\ninterface DedupEntry {\\n  hash: string;\\n  timestamp: number;\\n}\\n\\n// chatId → 最近的 dedup entries\\nconst _cache = new Map<number, DedupEntry[]>();\\n\\n/**\\n * 計算文字的去重 hash（取前 2000 字做 SHA-256 前 16 hex）。\\n * 用前 2000 字是因為 specialist summary 最長就 2000。\\n */\\nfunction computeHash(text: string): string {\\n  const input = text.slice(0, 2000);\\n  return createHash(\\\"sha256\\\").update(input, \\\"utf8\\\").digest(\\\"hex\\\").slice(0, 16);\\n}\\n\\n/**\\n * 檢查是否重複。若不重複則登記並回傳 false；若重複回傳 true。\\n *\\n * @param chatId — Telegram chat ID\\n * @param summary — specialist result summary 文字\\n * @returns true = 重複（應跳過注入）, false = 新的（可以注入）\\n */\\nexport function isDuplicateResult(chatId: number, summary: string): boolean {\\n  const now = Date.now();\\n  const hash = computeHash(summary);\\n\\n  // 取出或初始化 cache\\n  let entries = _cache.get(chatId);\\n  if (!entries) {\\n    entries = [];\\n    _cache.set(chatId, entries);\\n  }\\n\\n  // 清理過期 entries\\n  const cutoff = now - DEDUP_WINDOW_MS;\\n  const fresh = entries.filter((e) => e.timestamp > cutoff);\\n\\n  // 檢查是否有相同 hash\\n  const dup = fresh.some((e) => e.hash === hash);\\n  if (dup) {\\n    _cache.set(chatId, fresh);\\n    return true;   <facts>
    <fact>File upload handler in index.ts downloads Telegram files via bot.api.getFile and constructs URL using config.telegramToken and file.file_path</fact>
    <fact>sanitizeName() replaces filesystem-unsafe characters (backslash, forward slash, colon, asterisk, question mark, quotes, angle brackets, pipe, control chars x00-x1f) with underscore and truncates to 120 chars</fact>
    <fact>Downloaded files saved to uploads/ with pattern chatId-timestamp-sanitizedname where timestamp uses ISO format with punctuation replaced</fact>
    <fact>Relay file handler validates files against isUnderAllowedRoot before processing RELAY_DELEGATE/RELAY_INPUT/RELAY_OUTPUT attachments</fact>
    <fact>File download uses fetch with Telegram file URL then writeFile to uploads directory created via mkdir recursive:true</fact>
    <fact>Agent receives relative path (uploads/filename) rather than absolute path to maintain portability</fact>
  </facts>
  <narrative>File upload security architecture examined across index.ts and commands/relay.ts. When users upload photos/documents/audio/video via Telegram, bot.api.getFile retrieves file metadata including file_path (Telegram's internal storage path). The bridge constructs download URL as https://api.telegram.org/file/bot{token}/{file_path} and fetches to local uploads/ directory. Filename sanitization is critical: sanitizeName() strips all filesystem metacharacters that could cause directory traversal or command injection (backslash, forward slash, colon, wildcards, angle brackets for shell redirection, pipe for command chaining, and ASCII control characters). Truncation to 120 characters prevents path length issues on Windows (MAX_PATH 260 minus directory prefix). Final path pattern chatId-timestamp-sanitizedname ensures uniqueness without cryptographic randomness (timestamp collisions prevented by Telegram's sequential message delivery). The agent prompt receives relative path "uploads/..." rather than absolute path, maintaining working directory portability. Relay file handling adds second layer: isUnderAllowedRoot validation before processing delegate/input/output attachments prevents accepting files from outside approved directories.</narrative>
  <concepts>
    <concept>how-it-works</concept>
    <concept>pattern</concept>
  </concepts>

    <file>src/commands/relay.ts</file>
  </files_read>

</observation>

<observation>
  <type>discovery</type>
  <title>Process lifecycle managed via crash handlers and graceful shutdown hooks</title>
  <subtitle>uncaughtException and unhandledRejection exit with code 1 for supervisor restart, SIGINT/SIGTERM trigger graceful cleanup</subtitle>
  <facts>
    <fact>uncaughtException and unhandledRejection handlers log error then call process.exit(1) to trigger supervisor restart</fact>
    <fact>SIGINT and SIGTERM handlers invoke shutdown() function which stops scheduler, bot, and sessions before process.exit(0)</fact>
    <fact>sessions.shutdown() wrapped in withTimeout using config.memoryExtractTimeoutMs plus 30-second buffer</fact>
    <fact>Graceful shutdown sequence: scheduler.stop(), bot.stop(), sessions.shutdown() with timeout, stopPetApp(), exit(0)</fact>
    <fact>start.bat outer loop mentioned in comments as supervisor that restarts on exit code 1</fact>
    <fact>main() catch block exits with code 1 ensuring startup failures also trigger restart</fact>
  </facts>
  <narrative>Process lifecycle management implements fail-fast crash recovery with graceful shutdown capability. Last-resort crash handlers (uncaughtException, unhandledRejection) immediately log and exit(1) rather than attempting recovery—this prevents corrupted state from persisting and relies on external supervisor (start.bat or systemd) to restart clean. Comment explicitly states "exit non-zero so the outer start.bat loop (or any supervisor) restarts us." Graceful shutdown path (SIGINT/SIGTERM) invokes shutdown() which orchestrates coordinated cleanup: scheduler.stop() clears setTimeout timers preventing orphan scheduled runs, bot.stop() closes Telegram long-polling connection, sessions.shutdown() runs onBeforeClose hooks for memory extraction and session archiving with timeout wrapper (extract timeout + 30s grace), stopPetApp() tears down desktop pet WebSocket server. Memory extraction timeout is critical: without it, a hung LLM call during fact extraction would deadlock shutdown indefinitely. Each step wrapped in try-catch so partial failures don't prevent subsequent cleanup. Final exit(0) signals clean stop to supervisor.</narrative>
  <concepts>
    <concept>how-it-works</concept>
    <concept>pattern</concept>
  </concepts>

    <file>src/index.ts</file>
  </files_read>

</observation>

<observation>
  <type>discovery</type>
  <title>Scheduler defends against setTimeout overflow and non-finite runAt corruption</title>
  <subtitle>MAX_TIMEOUT_MS guard for 24-day overflow, NaN/Infinity detection prevents hot-loop and drops corrupted entries</subtitle>
  <facts>
    <fact>MAX_TIMEOUT_MS constant set to 2_147_483_647 (2^31-1 milliseconds, approximately 24.85 days) to prevent Node.js 32-bit timer overflow</fact>
    <fact>Schedules beyond MAX_TIMEOUT_MS armed via trampoline pattern: setTimeout for MAX_TIMEOUT_MS then re-check and re-arm</fact>
    <fact>arm() validates Number.isFinite(entry.runAt) before setTimeout to detect corrupted schedules.json entries</fact>
    <fact>Non-finite runAt (NaN/Infinity) triggers entry removal, persist, and user notification preventing hot-loop of setTimeout(NaN) clamped to ~1ms</fact>
    <fact>computeNextRunAt returning null or non-finite value after recurring entry fires also triggers removal with user notification</fact>
    <fact>Quiet hours feature delays scheduled runs by updating entry.runAt and re-arming, avoiding middle-of-night notifications</fact>
  </facts>
  <narrative>Scheduler implements defensive programming against multiple edge cases in delayed execution. Primary guard is MAX_TIMEOUT_MS = 2^31-1 (Integer.MAX_VALUE in Java, max safe value for Node setTimeout internals before 32-bit overflow). Schedules beyond 24.85 days use trampoline: arm a MAX_TIMEOUT_MS sleep, re-check entry still exists on wake, re-arm with remaining delay. This prevents overflow while supporting arbitrarily distant schedules. Second guard detects corrupted persistence: Number.isFinite(entry.runAt) check catches NaN (from JSON parsing undefined/null), Infinity (from arithmetic errors), or other non-numeric values. Without this check, setTimeout(NaN) would clamp to ~1ms per Node.js spec, causing hot-loop: fire immediately, find entry still exists, re-arm with same NaN, repeat. Fix is entry removal + persist + user notification explaining corrupted recurrence. Third guard in afterFire(): when recurring entry computes next tick, null or non-finite result (from malformed cron spec or DST edge case) also drops entry with notification. Comment explicitly states "Node clamps that to ~1ms and logs TimeoutNaNWarning, causing the scheduler to hot-loop firing the same entry forever." This defensive architecture ensures corrupted data fails safely rather than consuming CPU.</narrative>
  <concepts>
    <concept>how-it-works</concept>
    <concept>gotcha</concept>
    <concept>pattern</concept>
  </concepts>

    <file>src/scheduler.ts</file>
  </files_read>

</observation>

heartbeat every ${HEARTBEAT_MS / 1000}s, high-freq poller on demand (key=\\\"${redisKey(cfg.botName)}\\\")`);\\n}\\n\\nexport function stopRelayPoller(): void {\\n  if (pollTimer) {\\n    clearInterval(pollTimer);\\n    pollTimer = null;\\n  }\\n  if (heartbeatTimer) {\\n    clearInterval(heartbeatTimer);\\n    heartbeatTimer = null;\\n  }\\n}\\n\\n/** Quick connectivity test - SET + GET + DEL a test key. */\\nexport async function testRedisConnection(relayConfig: RelayConfig): Promise<boolean> {\\n  cfg = relayConfig;\\n  try {\\n    await redisCommand([\\\"SET\\\", \\\"relay:_ping\\\", \\\"pong\\\", \\\"EX\\\", \\\"10\\\"]);\\n    const { result } = await redisCommand([\\\"GET\\\", \\\"relay:_ping\\\"]);\\n    await redisCommand([\\\"DEL\\\", \\\"relay:_ping\\\"]);\\n    return result === \\\"pong\\\";\\n  } catch (err) {\\n    console.error(\\\"[relay] connection test failed:\\\", err);\\n    return false;\\n  } finally {\\n    cfg = relayConfig;\\n  }\\n}\\n\\n// ─── Dedup for \\\"both\\\" mode ─────────────────────────────────────────────────\\nconst recentHashes = new Set<string>();\\nconst DEDUP_TTL_MS = 10_000;\\n\\nfunction dedupKey(from: string, text: string): string {\\n  // Simple hash: first 100 chars of text + sender\\n  return `${from}:${text.slice(0, 100)}`;\\n}\\n\\n/** Returns true if this message is a duplicate (already seen recently). */\\nexport function isDuplicate(from: string, text: string): boolean {\\n  const key = dedupKey(from, text);\\n  if (recentHashes.has(key)) return true;\\n  recentHashes.add(key);\\n  setTimeout(() => recentHashes.delete(key), DEDUP_TTL_MS);\\n  return false;\\n}\\n\\n// ─── Telegram bot-to-bot incoming handler ──────────────────────────────────\\nconst RELAY_PREFIX_RE = /^\\\\[RELAY:([^\\\\]]+)\\\\]\\\\s*/;\\n\\nexport interface ParsedBotRelay {\\n  from: string;\\n  text: string;\\n}\\n\\n/** Parse an incoming bot message. Returns null if not a relay message. */\\nexport function parseBotRelayMessage(rawText: string): ParsedBotRelay | null {\\n  const m = RELAY_PREFIX_RE.exec(rawText);\\n  if (!m) return null;\\n  return { from: m[1], text: rawText.slice(m[0].length) };\\n}\\n\\n/** Get the registered incoming handler (set by startRelayPoller). */\\nexport function getRelayHandler(): ((msg: RelayMessage) => void) | null {\\n  return onIncoming;\\n}\\n\\n// ─── Structured Delegation Protocol ────────────────────────────────────────\\nexport const DELEGATE_PREFIX = \\\"[RELAY_DELEGATE]\\\";\\nexport const RESULT_PREFIX = \\\"[RELAY_RESULT]\\\";\\n\\nexport interface DelegatePayload {\\n  id: string;          // correlation_id\\n  goal: string;\\n  context?: string;\\n  maxTurns?: number;\\n}\\n\\nexport interface ResultPayload {\\n  id: string;          // correlation_id matching the delegation\\n  status: \\\"done\\\" | \\\"failed\\\" | \\\"timeout\\\";\\n  summary: string;\\n}\\n\\n/** Generate a correlation_id for delegation. */\\nexport function generateCorrelationId(): string {\\n  const hex = Math.random().toString(16).slice(2, 6);\\n  return `rd_${Date.now()}_${hex}`;\\n}\\n\\n/** Send a structured delegation request via relay. */\\nexport async function sendRelayDelegate(targetBot: string, payload: DelegatePayload): Promise<void> {\\n  const text = `${DELEGATE_PREFIX} ${JSON.stringify(payload)}`;\\n  await sendRelay(targetBot, text);\\n}\\n\\n/** Send a structured result back via relay. */\\nexport async function sendRelayResult(targetBot: string, payload: ResultPayload): Promise<void> {\\n  const text = `${RESULT_PREFIX} ${JSON.stringify(payload)}`;\\n  await sendRelay(targetBot, text);\\n}\\n\\n/** Parse incoming relay text — returns typed payload or null (plain message). */\\nexport function parseRelayPayload(text: string): \\n  | { type: \\\"delegate\\\"; payload: DelegatePayload }\\n  | { type: \\\"result\\\"; payload: ResultPayload }\\n  | null {\\n  if (text.startsWith(DELEGATE_PREFIX)) {\\n    try {\\n      const json = JSON.parse(text.slice(DELEGATE_PREFIX.length).trim());\\n      return { type: \\\"delegate\\\", payload: json as DelegatePayload };\\n    } catch { return null; }\\n  }\\n  if (text.startsWith(RESULT_PREFIX)) {\\n    try {\\n      const json = JSON.parse(text.slice(RESULT_PREFIX.length).trim());\\n      return { type: \\\"result\\\", payload: json as ResultPayload };\\n    } catch { return null; }\\n  }\\n  return null;\\n}\\n\",\"numLines\":442,\"startLine\":1,\"totalLines\":442}}"</outcome>

Hard-coded\\n// on purpose — the user chose \\\"default, no env var override; change here\\n// if it turns out to be wrong.\\\" See docs/ab-token-protocol.md §RESTART.\\n/** Minimum gap between two successful RESTARTs on the same goal. */\\nexport const GOAL_RESTART_COOLDOWN_MS = 5 * 60 * 1000;\\n/** Maximum number of RESTARTs a single goal can fire. */\\nexport const GOAL_RESTART_CAP_PER_GOAL = 5;\\n\\n/** Directory containing per-chat goal-state files. */\\nexport function goalStateDir(memoryDir: string): string {\\n  return path.join(memoryDir, \\\"goal-state\\\");\\n}\\n\\n/** Path to the goal-state file for one chat. */\\nexport function goalStatePath(memoryDir: string, chatId: number): string {\\n  return path.join(goalStateDir(memoryDir), `${chatId}.json`);\\n}\\n\\nfunction nowIso(): string {\\n  return new Date().toISOString();\\n}\\n\\nfunction errEmpty(chatId: number): Error {\\n  return new Error(`no goal state for chat ${chatId}`);\\n}\\n\\n// ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────\\n// Read\\n// ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────\\n\\n/** Current goal state for this chat, or null if no file exists. Corrupt\\n * files are treated as \\\"no goal\\\" (readJsonOrDefault returns null).\\n *\\n * Normalises legacy state files that pre-date the RESTART guardrail\\n * fields (last_restart_at / restart_count / restart_reasons) by filling\\n * in defaults on read. We intentionally do NOT write back here — the\\n * next real mutation (incrementTurn / markRestart / etc.) will persist\\n * the normalised shape, avoiding a write storm on bridge startup. */\\nexport async function get(\\n  memoryDir: string,\\n  chatId: number,\\n): Promise<GoalState | null> {\\n  const raw = await readJsonOrDefault<GoalState | null>(\\n    goalStatePath(memoryDir, chatId),\\n    () => null,\\n  );\\n  if (!raw || typeof raw !== \\\"object\\\") return null;\\n  if (raw._schema_version !== 1) return null;\\n  // Legacy shapes (pre-2026-05-02) don't have the RESTART fields. Cast\\n  // through unknown so TS lets us poke at possibly-absent fields.\\n  const legacy = raw as unknown as Partial<GoalState>;\\n  return {\\n    ...raw,\\n    last_restart_at:\\n      typeof legacy.last_restart_at === \\\"string\\\" ? legacy.last_restart_at : null,\\n    restart_count:\\n      typeof legacy.restart_count === \\\"number\\\" && legacy.restart_count >= 0\\n        ? legacy.restart_count\\n        : 0,\\n    restart_reasons: Array.isArray(legacy.restart_reasons)\\n      ? legacy.restart_reasons.filter((s): s is string => typeof s === \\\"string\\\")\\n      : [],\\n    reflection_retries:\\n      typeof legacy.reflection_retries === \\\"number\\\" && legacy.reflection_retries >= 0\\n        ? legacy.reflection_retries\\n        : 0,\\n    last_reflection:\\n      typeof legacy.last_reflection === \\\"string\\\" ? legacy.last_reflection : null,\\n  };\\n}\\n\\n/** True iff the chat has a goal currently in 'active' state. */\\nexport async function isActive(\\n  memoryDir: string,\\n  chatId: number,\\n): Promise<boolean> {\\n  const s = await get(memoryDir, chatId);\\n  return s?.status === \\\"active\\\";\\n}\\n\\n// ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────\\n// Mutations — all go through updateJson() for atomic read-modify-write\\n// ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────\\n\\n/** Start a new goal or overwrite a terminated one. DEFAULT_MAX_TURNS;\\n  if (maxTurns < 1) throw new Error(`maxTurns must be >= 1 (got ${maxTurns})`);\\n  const ts = nowIso();\\n\\n  // set() has special semantics: \\\"(no file) → active\\\" vs \\\"active/paused\\n  // → throw\\\" vs \\\"done/cleared → overwrite\\\". updateJson's read-modify-\\n  // write primitive doesn't fit — its `initial()` callback is how we'd\\n  // represent \\\"no file\\\", but the `update()` callback then receives that\\n  // initial value and can't tell it apart from a real active goal.\\n  //\\n  // So we do: read current state, apply rejection logic outside the\\n  // write chain, then atomicWriteJson the new state.\\n  const existing = await get(memoryDir, chatId);\\n  if (\\n    existing &&\\n    (existing.status === \\\"active\\\" || existing.status === \\\"paused\\\")\\n  ) {\\n    throw new Error(\\n      `chat ${chatId} already has a ${existing.status} goal; ` +\",\"numLines\":200,\"startLine\":1,\"totalLines\":573}}"</outcome>

  <outcome>"{\"type\":\"text\",\"file\":{\"filePath\":\"G:\\\\AI\\\\telegram-kiro-bridge-main\\\\src\\\\reply-dedup.ts\",\"content\":\"/**\\n * Reply Dedup — 防止 specialist/parallel delegate 結果重複注入主 session。\\n *\\n * 機制：對每個 (chatId, summaryHash) 做 5 分鐘時間窗去重。\\n * 如果同一個 chatId 在 5 分鐘內收到相同 summary hash 的結果，\\n * 視為重複並跳過注入。\\n *\\n * 觸發場景：\\n * - retry 邏輯導致 specialist 重跑同一任務\\n * - goal continuation 過程中重複觸發 delegate\\n * - 網路/timeout retry 導致結果雙重回傳\\n */\\nimport { createHash } from \\\"node:crypto\\\";\\n\\nconst DEDUP_WINDOW_MS = 5 * 60 * 1000; // 5 分鐘\\n\\ninterface DedupEntry {\\n  hash: string;\\n  timestamp: number;\\n}\\n\\n// chatId → 最近的 dedup entries\\nconst _cache = new Map<number, DedupEntry[]>();\\n\\n/**\\n * 計算文字的去重 hash（取前 2000 字做 SHA-256 前 16 hex）。\\n * 用前 2000 字是因為 specialist summary 最長就 2000。\\n */\\nfunction computeHash(text: string): string {\\n  const input = text.slice(0, 2000);\\n  return createHash(\\\"sha256\\\").update(input, \\\"utf8\\\").digest(\\\"hex\\\").slice(0, 16);\\n}\\n\\n/**\\n * 檢查是否重複。若不重複則登記並回傳 false；若重複回傳 true。\\n *\\n * @param chatId — Telegram chat ID\\n * @param summary — specialist result summary 文字\\n * @returns true = 重複（應跳過注入）, false = 新的（可以注入）\\n */\\nexport function isDuplicateResult(chatId: number, summary: string): boolean {\\n  const now = Date.now();\\n  const hash = computeHash(summary);\\n\\n  // 取出或初始化 cache\\n  let entries = _cache.get(chatId);\\n  if (!entries) {\\n    entries = [];\\n    _cache.set(chatId, entries);\\n  }\\n\\n  // 清理過期 entries\\n  const cutoff = now - DEDUP_WINDOW_MS;\\n  const fresh = entries.filter((e) => e.timestamp > cutoff);\\n\\n  // 檢查是否有相同 hash\\n  const dup = fresh.some((e) => e.hash === hash);\\n  if (dup) {\\n    _cache.set(chatId, fresh);\\n    return true; // 重複\\n  }\\n\\n  // 登記新 entry\\n  fresh.push({ hash, timestamp: now });\\n  _cache.set(chatId, fresh);\\n  return false; // 不重複\\n}\\n\\n/**\\n * 清除指定 chatId 的 dedup cache（例如 /reset 時）。\\n */\\nexport function clearDedupCache(chatId?: number): void {\\n  if (chatId !== undefined) {\\n    _cache.delete(chatId);\\n  } else {\\n    _cache.clear();\\n  }\\n}\\n\",\"numLines\":79,\"startLine\":1,\"totalLines\":79}}"</outcome>
